brandAggregates.headquartersRaw: London (1 Duchess Street, London, Greater London), United Kingdom
brandAggregates.description: Fix8 is a UK-based functional beverage brand focused on naturally fermented, probiotic sparkling drinks. Founded by Freya Twigden, Fix8 produces kombucha and (from 2025) water kefir made with real fruit, science-backed probiotic cultures and added Vitamin C. Products are marketed as low-calorie, plant-based, vegan, gluten-free and with no added sugar. The brand sells canned and bottled drinks through national retailers, online direct-to-consumer, and foodservice accounts.
brandAggregates.industry: Food & Beverage — Functional sparkling/probiotic beverages (kombucha, water kefir)
brandAggregates.additionalContext: this brand is manufacturer of products in these categories: Flavoured Sparkling Water, Sparkling Water, Fizzy Drinks, Soft Drinks, Vinegar Drinks
brandAggregates.archetypeAffinity: Fix8 is primarily positioned as a functional, ingredient-led beverage brand whose kombucha and upcoming water kefir products strongly appeal to health- and ingredient-conscious consumers. The brand’s core strengths—naturally fermented probiotic cultures, no added sugar, Vitamin C, plant-based and gluten-free credentials, real fruit flavours and multi-channel retail/DTC presence—drive high alignment with Wellness Seekers and Thoughtful Buyers, and provide good resonance with Experience Explorers ...
brandAggregates.researchOverallAssessment: Fix8 is a legitimate, active UK functional beverage brand founded in 2018, specialising in kombucha and expanding into water kefir. Evidence of legitimacy includes an active official website, retailer listings (Waitrose, Morrisons, Ocado, Selfridges), trade press coverage, LinkedIn presence, and noted commercial hires. Recent activity through 2024–2025 (national supermarket listings, product launches, and distribution growth) supports an 'active' status with very high confidence. Logo extraction...

brandAggregates.description: Double Dutch is a UK-based premium mixers and tonic brand founded by Dutch twins Joyce and Raissa de Haas. The company develops flavoured sparkling waters, tonic waters, cocktail mixers and sodas using flavour-pairing and molecular gastronomy techniques. Products are designed to enhance spirits (gin, vodka, tequila, rum, cognac) or be enjoyed standalone as non-alcoholic beverages. The brand emphasizes sustainability (B Corp / carbon neutral), flavour innovation, and services both on-trade (hotel...
brandAggregates.industry: Beverages — premium mixers, tonics and soft drinks

brandAggregates.archetypeAffinity: Double Dutch is positioned as a premium, flavour-driven mixer and sparkling beverage brand with clear sustainability credentials (B Corp, carbon neutral) and an experiential, craft-led product philosophy. Its strengths map strongly to Quality Seekers and Experience Explorers who value artisanal production, sophisticated flavour pairing, and on-trade tasting contexts. The Conscious Consumer also aligns well thanks to verified sustainability claims, while Trend Enthusiasts and Thoughtful Buyers sh...

brandAggregates.description: SKIP is a UK-based functional soft drinks brand producing cold-pressed CBD beverages and Lion's Mane mushroom drinks. The brand emphasises plant-based ingredients, no added sugar and recyclable packaging, positioning itself around doing 'better for you, and the planet.' Products are manufactured in the UK and sold direct-to-consumer. The CBD line uses cold-pressed extraction claimed to be up to 8x more effective than standard CBD isolate, with 5mg active CBD per 250ml can. All drinks are vegan, ...
brandAggregates.industry: Beverages — functional CBD & nootropic soft drinks
brandAggregates.additionalContext: Product range spans two lines: CBD drinks (Peach & Ginger, Lemon & Basil, Elderflower & Mint) and Lion's Mane drinks (Blood Orange, Cloudy Apple). Sold in 12/24/48-can multipacks at £20/£38/£72. Free delivery on all UK orders. All cans fully recyclable. Plant-based ingredients. Manufactured in the UK.
brandAggregates.archetypeAffinity: SKIP appeals most strongly to Wellness Seekers drawn to CBD's calming properties and the brand's plant-based, no-added-sugar credentials. Conscious Consumers respond to the vegan formulation, UK manufacturing and recyclable packaging. Experience Explorers are attracted to novel CBD and Lion's Mane functional ingredients as an alternative to conventional drinks. The brand's low price point and approachable flavours (peach-ginger, elderflower-mint) also resonate with Curious Tryers who want to exp...
brandAggregates.researchOverallAssessment: SKIP is a small, early-stage UK functional drinks brand with a live DTC website, active social media presence and a coherent product range across CBD and Lion's Mane categories. Legitimacy indicators include consistent branding, real product photography, transparent ingredient lists and functional Shopify checkout. No retail listings, trade press coverage or external validation identified. Brand appears genuine but pre-scale — typical of a bootstrapped DTC startup in the functional beverages spa...

brandAggregates.description: UNAI is a UK-crafted adaptogenic soft drinks brand producing low-sugar, all-natural lightly sparkling beverages infused with nootropics and adaptogens including Lion's Mane, Cordyceps, Reishi and Ashwagandha. Founded by childhood friends reconnected through a shared love of music and the outdoors, the brand is built around 'conscious connection' — helping consumers pause, connect and stay grounded. Products are 100% natural with no artificial flavours.
brandAggregates.additionalContext: Two-product range: UPLIFT (Pink Grapefruit & Strawberry with Lion's Mane + Cordyceps for focus/energy) and UNWIND (Lemon & Lavender with Reishi + Ashwagandha for relaxation/sleep). Sold DTC at £29.95 per 12-pack with 15% subscription discount. Also available at RAYE Store pop-up in Notting Hill. Brand hosts experiential events (hikes, cold plunge sessions) emphasising conscious connection.
brandAggregates.archetypeAffinity: UNAI's strongest alignment is with Wellness Seekers, who are drawn to the functional mushroom and adaptogen ingredients (Lion's Mane, Cordyceps, Reishi, Ashwagandha) and the brand's low-sugar, all-natural formulation. Experience Explorers connect with the brand's experiential events (hikes, cold plunges) and novel ingredient combinations (prickly pear, burdock root). Conscious Consumers appreciate the 100% natural, no-artificial-flavours positioning and the mindfulness-centred brand ethos. The '...
brandAggregates.researchOverallAssessment: UNAI is a very early-stage UK adaptogenic drinks brand with a functional website, two products and minimal distribution (DTC + one pop-up location). Legitimacy indicators include a professional website with real product photography, transparent ingredient lists, nutritional information per 100ml, functional checkout and active Instagram/TikTok accounts. No retail listings, trade press coverage, regulatory certifications or external validation identified. The brand appears genuine but extremely e...

brandAggregates.headquartersRaw: London, England, United Kingdom (registered office: 13 Hillgate Street, London W8 7SP)
brandAggregates.description: TRIP is a functional beverage brand producing lightly sparkling, non-alcoholic drinks, powders and supplements (gummies) formulated to promote calm and wellbeing. Products are flavoured sparkling waters/soft drinks infused with magnesium, adaptogens (e.g. Lion's Mane, Ashwagandha, L-Theanine), botanicals and, historically, CBD variants. The company positions its range as an alcohol alternative and a "calm" beverage category for everyday stress relief, sells via D2C and major retailers, and also ...
brandAggregates.industry: Beverages — Soft Drinks / Sparkling Water / Functional Beverages
brandAggregates.additionalContext: this brand is manufacturer of products in these categories: Flavoured Sparkling Water, Sparkling Water, Fizzy Drinks, Soft Drinks, Liqueurs
brandAggregates.archetypeAffinity: TRIP is a functional beverage brand clearly oriented toward wellness-focused and trend-driven consumers: its core positioning as a 'calm' alcohol alternative, formulations containing adaptogens and magnesium, and product expansion into powders and gummies make it an excellent fit for Wellness Seekers and Trend Enthusiasts. The brand also attracts Quality Seekers and Experience Explorers who appreciate curated ingredient blends and sensory variety, and performs well with On-the-Go and Impulse sho...
brandAggregates.researchOverallAssessment: TRIP is a legitimate, active functional beverage brand founded in 2019 and headquartered in London, UK. Evidence from the brand's official site, press releases (PR Newswire), industry coverage (BeverageDaily), business databases (PitchBook, Tracxn) and retail distribution announcements supports high confidence in the brand's authenticity and market activity. The company has rapidly scaled through D2C and major retail partnerships, launched functional product lines (magnesium/adaptogen drinks, gu...

brandAggregates.description: DASH refers to two distinct, legitimate beverage and seasoning brands that share the name. 1) Dash (formerly Mrs. Dash) is a long-standing U.S. salt-free seasoning line of herb-and-spice blends and mixes (blends like Original, Lemon Pepper, Taco Seasoning, grilling blends), marketed to consumers seeking bold flavor without added sodium; the line emphasizes heart-healthy, low-sodium eating and is marketed by B&G Foods. 2) Dash Water (DASH) is a UK-founded sparkling spring water / flavoured sparkl...
brandAggregates.industry: Food & Beverage (seasonings, spices, and flavored sparkling water)
brandAggregates.additionalContext: this brand is manufacturer of products in these categories: Salt, Flavoured Sparkling Water, Sparkling Water, Seasonings & Spices, Herbs & Spices
brandAggregates.archetypeAffinity: DASH encompasses two distinct food & beverage businesses — Mrs. Dash (salt-free seasoning line, mainstream U.S. pantry brand owned by B&G Foods) and Dash Water (UK-founded sparkling spring water using real and surplus fruit with a sustainability/food-waste story). Taken together, the portfolio most strongly aligns with wellness-oriented and health-conscious consumers because Mrs. Dash’s low-sodium, flavour-forward seasoning and Dash Water’s zero-calorie, unsweetened fruit-infused sparkling water...
brandAggregates.researchOverallAssessment: DASH is a real, active market name used by at least two separate food & beverage businesses: Dash (formerly Mrs. Dash) is an established U.S. salt-free seasoning brand with documented history since the early 1980s (commercially introduced 1983) and currently marketed by B&G Foods; Dash Water is a UK-founded flavored sparkling water brand launched within the last several years focused on using real/wonky fruit and sustainability, with rapid retail growth in the UK. Both brands have active web pro...

brandAggregates.headquartersRaw: Manchester, England, United Kingdom (primary addresses reported: 9 Great Ancoats Street, Manchester M4 5AD; Department Bonded Warehouse, 18 Lower Byrom Street, Manchester M3 4AP)
brandAggregates.description: Hip Pop is a UK-based beverage brand that produces lightly sparkling, fruit-flavoured drinks (kombucha-style and sodas) formulated with real fruit, live cultures and no artificial sweeteners. Launched by founders Emma Thackray and Kenny Goodman, Hip Pop positions itself in the functional/‘better-for-you’ soft drinks space with products including fruit-flavoured sodas, kombucha variants and CBD kombucha. The brand emphasises gut-friendly benefits, natural ingredients, bold packaging and retail ex...
brandAggregates.industry: Food & Beverage (functional soft drinks / non-alcoholic beverages)
brandAggregates.additionalContext: this brand is manufacturer of products in these categories: Fruit-Flavoured Drinks, Fizzy Drinks, Flavoured Sparkling Water, Vinegar Drinks, Sparkling Water
brandAggregates.archetypeAffinity: Hip Pop is positioned primarily as a functional, better-for-you beverage brand with strong appeal to wellness-oriented and trend-driven consumers. Its product set—kombucha-style drinks, live cultures, real fruit and no artificial sweeteners—aligns exceptionally well with Wellness Seekers and strongly with Trend Enthusiasts and Thoughtful Buyers who value ingredient transparency. The brand’s bold packaging and unique variants create discovery and impulse potential, making it a reasonable fit for ...

brandAggregates.description: Agua de Madre is a London-based producer of fermented beverages, primarily craft-brewed water kefir. The brand makes organic, vegan, naturally sparkling, low-sugar drinks packed with live probiotic cultures (water kefir), plus a Remedio range (fermented ginger/fruit infusions) and probiotic culture shots. brandAggregates.archetypeAffinity: Agua de Madre is positioned as a mindful, small-batch functional beverage brand that strongly appeals to consumers who prioritise sustainability, organic/vegan credentials and gut-health benefits. brandAggregates.description: Dalston's is a beverage company known for producing 'Soda With Soul,' which is full-flavor fizz made with real fruit and no added sugar or artificial sweeteners. The brand emphasizes sustainability, using recycled aluminum cans that are 100% recyclable. It was created by chefs in an East London nightclub.
brandAggregates.archetypeAffinity: Dalston's positions itself at the intersection of craft flavour, clean ingredients and sustainability: a chef-origin story, real fruit flavours, and recyclable aluminium cans define the brand. This makes it an exceptional match for Conscious Consumers and a strong fit for Wellness Seekers who value no added sugar and natural ingredients. brandAggregates.researchOverallAssessment: Dalston's is a legitimate and active brand known for its commitment to quality and sustainability in the beverage industry. productAggregates.description: This Fix8 Kombucha offers a refreshing and fruity experience, blending the sweet notes of strawberry with aromatic basil. Slow-brewed and enriched with live cultures, it supports gut health and provides 25% of your daily recommended Vitamin C, making it a delicious and beneficial sparkling beverage.
productAggregates.ingredients: Filtered water; Kombucha cultures; Sencha green tea; sugar; natural flavouring; 2% strawberry juice from concentrate; purple carrot concentrate; natural basil flavouring; Vitamin C; lactic acid bacillus Produced in a manufactuer that is nut; soy and dairy free.

productAggregates.description: UNWIND — a lightly sparkling adaptogenic drink infused with Reishi mushroom and Ashwagandha to ease the mind and promote relaxation. Blends lemon and lavender with prickly pear juice, turmeric, burdock root and artichoke leaf. Enriched with magnesium, L-tryptophan and vitamin B12. 100% natural, only 13 kcal per 100ml.
productAggregates.ingredients: Sparkling Water, Grape Juice from Concentrate, Prickly Pear Juice, Reishi Mushroom, Turmeric, Ashwagandha, Burdock Root, Artichoke Leaf, Citric Acid, Magnesium Citrate, Natural Flavourings (Lemon & Lavender), L-Tryptophan, Ascorbic Acid, Steviol Glycosides, Vitamin B12 (Cyanocobalamin)

targetUsers.motivation: Ashwagandha and Reishi have growing mainstream recognition for stress/anxiety relief — a drink format makes it easy and pleasant
L-tryptophan and magnesium are recognised sleep-support ingredients — combined with adaptogens in a bedtime drink
Lavender-lemon flavour profile and calming function serves as an evening 'treat' drink without alcohol

productAggregates.description: A sparkling CBD-infused drink combining juicy peach with warming ginger and botanical extracts. Cold-pressed CBD preserves the full spectrum of cannabinoids for maximum efficacy. Contains 5mg active CBD per 250ml can, real fruit juice and calming botanicals including chamomile, green tea and lemon balm.

productAggregates.description: UPLIFT — a lightly sparkling adaptogenic drink blending pink grapefruit and strawberry with Lion's Mane and Cordyceps mushroom extracts to support focus and energy. 100% natural with no artificial flavours. Designed for mornings, afternoon slumps or pre-adventure fuel. Only 12 kcal per 100ml.
productAggregates.ingredients: Sparkling Water, Grape Juice from Concentrate, Natural Flavourings (Pink Grapefruit & Strawberry), Botanical Extracts (Lemon Balm, Rooibos, Lion's Mane, Cordyceps, Panax Ginseng, Green Tea), Citric Acid, Ascorbic Acid, Black Carrot Extract, Steviol Glycosides

targetUsers.motivation: Want sustained mental clarity from adaptogens rather than the spike-crash cycle of coffee or energy drinks
Cordyceps for athletic performance + Lion's Mane for focus makes this a dual-purpose functional drink
Brand ethos of 'conscious connection' and 'pause, connect, stay grounded' resonates with mindfulness values

productAggregates.description: This Fix8 Kombucha is a refreshing, low-calorie sparkling drink infused with Sicilian orange and delicate saffron. It's packed with live cultures and Vitamin C to support immune health, making it a functional and delicious plant-based beverage. Enjoy it chilled for a bold and invigorating experience.
productAggregates.ingredients: Filtered Water, Kombucha Cultures, Sencha Green Tea, Sugar, Natural Flavouring, Sicilian Orange Extract, Saffron Extract, Vitamin C, Lactic Acid Bacillus

targetUsers.motivation: Seeks tasty, science-backed drinks that support gut health and provide a low-calorie alternative to alcohol or sugary sodas
Wants a social, flavorful beverage that feels premium and delivers perceived health benefits without alcohol
Looks for products with live cultures and added vitamin C to support digestion and immune health

companionProducts.type: Probiotic Yogurt or Dairy-free Probiotic Dessert

productAggregates.description: A delicate sparkling CBD drink pairing floral elderflower with cool mint and calming botanicals. Cold-pressed CBD at 5mg per can for gentle relaxation. Made with real fruit juice from apple and grape, plus chamomile, green tea and lemon balm extracts.
productAggregates.ingredients: Carbonated Water, Fruit Juices from Concentrate 17% (Apple, Grape), Flavour Enhancer (Erythritol), Natural Flavour Elderflower, Botanical Extracts (Chamomile, Mint 0.07%, Green Tea, Lemon Balm), Acid (Citric Acid), Natural Flavouring, Cold-Pressed CBD Extract (5mg/250ml), Sweetener (Steviol Glycosides from Stevia)

targetUsers.motivation: Gentle, floral flavour profile appeals to those who find other CBD drinks too bold
The calming flavour combination (elderflower, mint, chamomile) signals relaxation
Elderflower-mint is an accessible, familiar flavour that lowers CBD trial barriers

productAggregates.description: This FIX8 Ginger Turmeric Kombucha is a refreshing, plant-based sparkling drink packed with live cultures and Vitamin C to support immune health. It's low in calories and made with natural ingredients, including ginger, turmeric, and black pepper, scientifically designed to reach your gut. Enjoy it cold for a bold and healthy beverage experience.

Need on-the-go beverages that provide a perceived health benefit (probiotics, vitamin C) and fit a hectic schedule
Prefer beverages that are explicitly plant-based, contain live cultures, and align with dietary choices

productAggregates.description: A premium, spicy ginger beer crafted for bold mixer use or on‑its‑own refreshment. Made with multiple ginger varieties for layered heat and depth, it pairs well with rum, bourbon and vodka and is bottled in a single‑serve 200ml glass bottle. Ingredients emphasize natural flavourings and sugar from sugar beet for a clean, effervescent finish.
productAggregates.ingredients: Carbonated Water, Sugar Beet, Natural Flavourings, Acid (Tartaric Acid)

targetUsers.motivation: Seeks vibrant, reliable mixers that enhance spirits and add layered ginger flavour without artificial ingredients
Wants a mixer that complements premium spirits and delivers consistent taste in mixed drinks
Needs a recognizable, premium mixer that elevates cocktail menus and is easy to portion and serve

usageOccasions.scenario: Premium cocktail mixer for at‑home cocktails

Enjoy this dairy-free, no-added-sugar beverage as a healthy everyday option.

targetUsers.motivation: Looking for a tasty, naturally fermented drink that provides live cultures and can be offered as a healthier alternative to sugary soft drinks.

usageOccasions.scenario: With meals as a refreshing beverage

productAggregates.description: Agua de Madre Blood Orange Water Kefir is a naturally fermented beverage made from a 2000-year-old Mexican Opuntia cactus culture. This dairy-free and caffeine-free drink is bursting with billions of probiotic live cultures, supporting digestive health. It's crafted entirely from plants in East London, offering a refreshing and invigorating experience.
productAggregates.ingredients: Kefir Culture Fermented in Filtered Water, Figs, Organic Cane Sugar, Lemons, Ginger, Blood Orange Syrup, Aromatic Bitters, Apple Cider Vinegar, Bitter Orange Extract, Bacillus Subtilis and Himalayan Salt

targetUsers.motivation: Looking for a tasty, socially acceptable probiotic drink that supports digestion and fits a mindful, low-alcohol lifestyle.
Desire for sophisticated, low-ABV or alcohol-free alternatives for social occasions and cocktail mixing.
Want convenient, pre-portioned probiotic options that are dairy-free, vegan and lower in sugar for family consumption.

usageOccasions.scenario: On-the-go refreshment for picnics and outings

productAggregates.description: Agua de Madre Pomegranate & Hibiscus Water Kefir is a naturally fermented beverage, crafted in East London from a 2000-year-old Mexican Opuntia cactus culture. This dairy-free and caffeine-free drink is bursting with billions of probiotic live cultures, supporting digestive health. Enjoy this invigorating drink as a refreshing and gut-friendly option.
productAggregates.ingredients: Kefir Culture Fermented in Filtered Water, Figs*, Cane Sugar*, Lemons*, Ginger*, Pomegranate & Hibiscus*, Apple Cider Vinegar*, Extracts of Pomegranate* & Hibiscus*, Bacillus Subtilis* & Himalayan Salt, (*Organic)

targetUsers.motivation: Seek probiotic-rich, low-intervention drinks to support gut health and replace sugary sodas.
Desire a sophisticated, fermented alternative to alcoholic beverages that still provides flavor and mouthfeel.
Want a flavorful, probiotic option that reduces soda intake for children and the household.

insightFeature.description: Compares structured product and brand positioning signals with transcript-backed reviewer mentions. It highlights validated claims, under-discussed claims, and emergent reviewer themes.

method.structuredPositioningScore: Share of a brand's products matching the dimension's structured keywords, with a small boost when brand-level intelligence also matches.
method.reviewerMentionRate: Share of transcript-backed reviews containing mapped explainable signals.
method.baseline: All transcript-backed reviews in the dataset.

transcriptUserEnriched.transcriptText: Here is our first one in the head to head mixer. We are going for a classic lemonade. We got Schweppes here, and we have our Double Dutch Premium Lemonade here. We have, for both, the exact same glass, one shot of vodka, so it's nice and plain. I thought I'd go with that one because then I really get the flavors rather than the taste of the alcohol. So it's very weak. So let's see. Double Dutch. Schweppes. There we go. I'll try Schweppes first. It's a common one I drink. I've drank many flavors of it, so I know I like it already. Very fresh, very light, very airy, gentle. Let's try Double Dutch. I've never tried any of these yet. This is the first one. Let's see if this one goes just as good. It's different. It's slightly bitter, but not in a bad way. In comparison, it's almost like Schweppes has this kind of lemony artificial aftertaste, whereas the Double Dutch doesn't have that. It's got a more natural refreshing refined taste that isn't as strong, but I actually prefer that because it's more gentle and more natural. So I think in round one, our winner is Double Dutch. Let's see how round two goes.
transcriptUserEnriched.summary: The reviewer compares two lemonades, Schweppes and Double Dutch, mixed with vodka. They find Schweppes to be fresh and light but note an artificial aftertaste. In contrast, Double Dutch is described as having a more natural, refined taste, which the reviewer prefers. The reviewer declares Double Dutch the winner of the first round.

transcriptUserEnriched.transcriptText: Hi. One of my favorite drinks is gin, and today I'm quite excited to be doing a head to head mixer challenge with one of my favorite gins, London gin. And elderflower and cucumber mixer from Franklin and Sons, and Double Dutch cucumber and watermelon. I'm gonna see which I like best, but first of all, let's prepare our glasses. So I've got some ice in here, equal amount of ice, and I'm gonna pour equal amounts of gin. And then let's do the mixer. So as I said, first one's Franklin and sons, and that's elderflower and cucumber. So that's that one. And then we've got Double Dutch. They're both 200 mil mixes, and I really like the bottle of the Double Dutch one. Right. And now for the taste test. So this is a Franklin. I can really taste the cucumber in that, but I have to say I think the elderflower makes it a little bit bitter. Oh, the watermelon and cucumber is delicious. Very refreshing and definitely hands down beats the Franklin and Son.
transcriptUserEnriched.summary: The reviewer conducts a mixer challenge comparing two drinks: Franklin and Sons' elderflower and cucumber mixer and Double Dutch's cucumber and watermelon mixer. After preparing the drinks, the reviewer tastes both, noting that while the Franklin and Sons mixer has a strong cucumber flavor, it is slightly bitter due to the elderflower. In contrast, the Double Dutch mixer is described as delicious and refreshing, ultimately being favored over the Franklin and Sons mixer.

transcriptUserEnriched.transcriptText: Hi, guys. Today, I'm testing the Fever Tree Pink Grapefruit Soda, and I'm going to be comparing it to the Double Dutch Pink Grapefruit Mixer. So let's give it a go. Okay. First, the soda. Nice, light, sparkly. The bubbles disappear straight away. Oh it's very tangy. I love the grapefruit flavour. I can feel my taste buds getting sour here and the smell of it is really lovely sweet and rosy. I really like that. There's not a lot of it in there, 150 mil. I think I'd like a little bit more but apart from that, yeah, it's really refreshing. It's really zesty. I really like that. Okay. So next we're gonna go for the Double Dutch. Nice little fizz. Oh it smells absolutely amazing like a real natural grapefruit flavour. Pour about 150 the same as the fever tree. Yeah it's beautiful really zesty smelling. The bubbles are a lot subtler. The taste is subtler as well. I think I prefer the more sour zesty flavor of the Fever Tree but this actually tastes quite natural which I like. It doesn't have as much flavor as the Fever Tree but I do like it. It's very hard to pick a winner. I think if the Double Dutch had a little bit more sort of zest and juiciness, I would definitely choose Double Dutch, but I'm afraid it goes to the Fever Tree. Well done Fever Tree. I really love the flavour.
transcriptUserEnriched.summary: The reviewer tests and compares Fever Tree Pink Grapefruit Soda and Double Dutch Pink Grapefruit Mixer. They find the Fever Tree soda to be tangy, refreshing, and flavorful, although they wish for a larger serving size. The Double Dutch has a natural grapefruit flavor and a subtler taste but lacks the zestiness of Fever Tree. Ultimately, the reviewer prefers Fever Tree for its stronger flavor, though they appreciate the natural taste of Double Dutch.

transcriptUserEnriched.transcriptText: Taste test time. I've got myself some vodka. I'm actually not testing the vodka. I'm testing the mixers. So I've got some Indian tonic water to try. I've always been a fan of tonic water. So I have got Double Dutch premium mixer Indian tonic water to try, and I've got the London essence original Indian tonic water to try too. Haven't heard of either of To me tonic water is tonic water, so we'll see if there's any difference. Right. I'm gonna start with the Double Dutch one. Yeah. That's nice enough. And now the London essence. Well, I don't know. That one seems to have a bit of a taste to it. Maybe it's because I had that one first. I'm gonna wash my mouth out, then I'm gonna try these a little bit on their own. Right. I'll do the London Essence one first this time. Actually, both of them on their own without the vodka, there's not much taste at all. Normally, Tonic Malt was really tangy. You know, you get a real taste of it. Let's go back to the vodka. And an essence. Double Dutch. Oh, now that one tastes stronger. Oh, I don't know. Oh, this is really hard. I don't think I've got a preference on this one. Okay. We're gonna go with either or on this one. I haven't got a favourite. I've seen neither in the shop. I don't know the brands So I'll probably just buy my normal one, the cheapies from the supermarket. I don't know. But if these are on offer when I'm out in the pub or whatever, we'll give it a go. No preference. Sorry.
transcriptUserEnriched.summary: The reviewer conducts a taste test of two brands of Indian tonic water: Double Dutch and London Essence. They express a general fondness for tonic water but find both brands to be similar in taste, with neither standing out as a favorite. The reviewer indicates that they would likely stick to their usual, cheaper tonic water unless these brands are on offer.

transcriptUserEnriched.transcriptText: Today, we have a standoff between the two ginger beers, Fentiman's classic ginger beer here, which we are all quite familiar with, I do believe, and the Double Dutch ginger beer, which I've not seen this one before, but I must say the packaging looks absolutely amazing. Love the fact that it's natural flavors apparently, which is quite cool. A glass for each of them and one piece of ice because I don't like too much ice, but a a little bit is okay. This one obviously is a lot more cloudy, and this one is a lot clearer. Very strong ginger smell. Not as strong as a smell from this one, but obviously, is natural flavoring, so I'm not expecting it to be very potent. I do like the flavor of this one when it first goes into your mouth. However, when you swallow it, the aftertaste it leaves is a very strong ginger taste, almost as if you have just swallowed a piece of ginger. Oh, wow. Okay. That is really nice. Also, I've noticed this is a certified b corporation, which I absolutely love. This one has a very strong aftertaste. It's not like I dislike the flavor of it, but I do think that the ginger taste at least in your throat is a bit too much. The Double Dutch is a lot sweeter. I I think it's a bit sweeter. It doesn't leave the aftertaste in your mouth. It's very pleasant. You can imagine when you're drinking these with a spirit, the aftertaste might be a bit less. Obviously, I don't really drink alcohol, so I'm not going to try that. I think the Double Dutch actually is my favorite because it tastes more natural. It doesn't leave that aftertaste in your mouth. It tastes a little bit sweeter, and it just tastes more palatable and enjoyable. Whereas this ginger beer tastes very strong, and it leaves that real kick of ginger in your throat, which I'm not a huge fan of, whereas the Double Dutch one doesn't really do that.
transcriptUserEnriched.summary: The review compares two ginger beers: Fentiman's classic ginger beer and Double Dutch ginger beer. The reviewer notes that Fentiman's has a strong ginger flavor and a potent aftertaste, while Double Dutch is sweeter, more palatable, and leaves no aftertaste. The reviewer prefers Double Dutch for its natural taste and overall enjoyment, highlighting its appealing packaging and certification as a B corporation.

transcriptUserEnriched.transcriptText: Hi, everyone. It's Luan here, I have been chosen by what she must try it to test some mixes to go with gin today, which I'm really excited about because I absolutely love a gin. This is my favorite one. It's the Whitney Mill Japanese yuzu and white strawberry. It's delicious if you haven't tried it. So I've got my two glasses here, my gin glasses, and I've just poured a measuring both, and I've got my diamond ice cubes and a slice of lemon. And then I've got the Double Dutch premium mixer, refreshing lemonade, low in calories. And then I've also got the London Essence crafted lemonade sparkling, and one's in a bottle, one's in a can. I'm not sure if that's gonna make any difference. So let's do a taste test. Okay. Well, that one is quite sharp, actually. That's taking me by surprise. Yeah. It's nice. It's very citrusy. It's very sharp, quite tangy, but nice. It goes really well with the gin. Let's try the London essence one. That one's quite sharp too, actually. Neither of them are particularly sweet. I think the London essence one is probably a little sweeter and a little bit less sharp. Both of them are a really lovely accompaniment to a gin. I'm not a massive fan of gin and tonics. I like my drinks quite sweet. So, yeah, I'm definitely a gin and lemonade girl, but both of these were great. Not too much difference between them. This one's just a little bit more sharp. So if you like a citrus sharp tang to your, drinks, then I'll go for this one. If you prefer a mild, slightly sweet one, I'd go for this one, but they're both lovely. Thank you.
transcriptUserEnriched.summary: Luan reviews two mixers for gin: Double Dutch premium mixer (refreshing lemonade) and London Essence crafted lemonade. She enjoys both, noting that they pair well with her favorite gin, Whitney Mill Japanese yuzu and white strawberry. The Double Dutch mixer is described as sharp and tangy, while the London Essence is slightly sweeter and milder. Luan prefers sweet drinks and favors gin and lemonade over gin and tonic. Overall, she finds both mixers to be great accompaniments to gin, with minor differences in taste.

I love cucumber and watermelon, I'm hoping it's gonna be a refreshing, taste and something that I can, have, as an alternative to some of the other fizzy drinks with sugar, etcetera. So I'm gonna give it a go. I'm gonna add mine to, a little toy story cup, and I've got some, reusable ice cream in there because I think it's gonna be extra nice if it's freezing cold. So I'm gonna give it a go. You can hear the kids having fun out in the sun. So it's a proper old school, a beautiful glass bottle, which I'm actually gonna keep and add some little flowers or something in and recycle it because it is lovely. transcriptUserEnriched.transcriptText: It's the first test of my mixer versus mixer, and I have the Schweppes in me in tonic water and the Double Dutch in me in tonic water. I love this bottle so much. I'm not a ton of water fan. I have had the shrubs before, but, like, couldn't really tell you the exact taste of it. And I'm gonna be mixing it with Robinson's Fruit Creations zesty orange and mango Because I think as much as I don't like the taste, I just love squash. Squash with anything. So we'll see how that is. I'm doing this on the floor. I watch my show. Got my two glasses, same amount of squash in each, and now I'm gonna add my tonic. Try it by itself first. Yeah. So round one, I would say Schweppes is the winner, Double Dutch. Got some catching up to do.

transcriptUserEnriched.transcriptText: Everyone. My name is Debbie, and today on watch humans, I'm back with some beautiful tonic to try with some beautiful gin. So first up, we've got the Double Dutch lemonade as you can see in this beautiful bottle, and then we've got the Schweppes lemonade in the can here. So first impressions, obviously, this one has a more elegant and beautiful and eye catching bottle. transcriptUserEnriched.transcriptText: I'm having a quiet night in, and my usual go to drink is a little weird, so don't judge me. But it is clear tequila and ginger beer. Trust me on this. Now I've tried a million different ginger beers. You've probably heard of London Essence. This fever tree is, like, the ones that you get the most in pubs as well as Ventamin's, I suppose. Have you heard of Double Dutch? I haven't. I'm gonna try them both and see which one I prefer. So that's really light, and that's lovely, and that's really easy to set. Let me try it by itself as well. Okay. It's very carbonated and not much flavor, and then it's got that fiery ginger kick at the end. So, like, the first initial drink, it's quite tasteless and just carbonated, which I don't like. I like flavor. But you can't really tell that when you've got it with a spirit because that's that's lovely. Second one is from Double Dutch. And first impressions is that I really like this bottle. I think it's really pretty. Can I open it? Whoops. That tastes different. That's definitely stronger because I can't taste the tequila as much. I mean, know it's there, but it's not as strong. Let me try that by itself. Oh, that is something I can easily drink by itself and I would enjoy. Whereas this, I would only ever have as a mixer, and I wouldn't enjoy it by itself. This is lovely by itself. Oh my goodness. The only other ginger beer that I drink by itself is old Jamaican, you know, like the cans. Much different price points, and that one is very fiery on purpose. This one tastes a little bit more special. This is nice. This is very nice. Cheers.

They describe the London Essence as light and carbonated but lacking flavor, making it suitable only as a mixer. In contrast, they find the Double Dutch ginger beer to be stronger, more flavorful, and enjoyable to drink on its own. The reviewer appreciates the aesthetic of the Double Dutch bottle and concludes that it tastes more special than the other brand.

transcriptUserEnriched.transcriptText: Today, we have a standoff between the two ginger beers, Fentiman's classic ginger beer here, which we are all quite familiar with, I do believe, and the Double Dutch ginger beer, which I've not seen this one before, but I must say the packaging looks absolutely amazing. Love the fact that it's natural flavors apparently, which is quite cool. A glass for each of them and one piece of ice because I don't like too much ice, but a a little bit is okay. This one obviously is a lot more cloudy, and this one is a lot clearer. Very strong ginger smell. Not as strong as a smell from this one, but obviously, is natural flavoring, so I'm not expecting it to be very potent. I do like the flavor of this one when it first goes into your mouth. However, when you swallow it, the aftertaste it leaves is a very strong ginger taste, almost as if you have just swallowed a piece of ginger. Oh, wow. Okay. That is really nice. Also, I've noticed this is a certified b corporation, which I absolutely love. This one has a very strong aftertaste. It's not like I dislike the flavor of it, but I do think that the ginger taste at least in your throat is a bit too much. The Double Dutch is a lot sweeter. I I think it's a bit sweeter. It doesn't leave the aftertaste in your mouth. It's very pleasant. You can imagine when you're drinking these with a spirit, the aftertaste might be a bit less. Obviously, I don't really drink alcohol, so I'm not going to try that. I think the Double Dutch actually is my favorite because it tastes more natural. It doesn't leave that aftertaste in your mouth. It tastes a little bit sweeter, and it just tastes more palatable and enjoyable. Whereas this ginger beer tastes very strong, and it leaves that real kick of ginger in your throat, which I'm not a huge fan of, whereas the Double Dutch one doesn't really do that.
transcriptUserEnriched.summary: The video review compares two ginger beers: Fentiman's classic ginger beer and Double Dutch ginger beer. The reviewer notes that Fentiman's has a strong ginger flavor and a potent aftertaste, which they find overwhelming. In contrast, Double Dutch is described as sweeter, more pleasant, and lacking a strong aftertaste, making it the reviewer's preferred choice. The reviewer appreciates the natural flavors in Double Dutch and its appealing packaging, while also acknowledging Fentiman's as a familiar option.

transcriptUserEnriched.transcriptText: Hey, everyone. My name is Debbie, and today on Watch Humans, I'm really excited because we're gonna be doing something a little bit different. We get to try a beautiful gin with some beautiful tonics. So the tonics that we've actually got today is the Double Dutch, and it is the cucumber and watermelon flavor that you can see there. And we're gonna try that alongside the Franklin London and Sons elderflower and cucumber tonic, and we can see what the two tastes like and compare them. Now in terms of the bottles themselves, they're both really stunning and really eye catching. I think this one, because of the design of this one, it just draws the eyes slightly more, but they are both still really beautiful bottles. Now I've got some really nice glasses here. As you can see, I've already added some ice and I'm also gonna add two slices of lemon and then we're gonna also add the gin. So the gin that I've got to try with it today is a Care Maroon gin that I really do like, and it's a Scottish gin. There Nice we and easy. So I'm just gonna add to that one and add about the same. This first one is the Double Dutch cucumber watermelon, and I've never tried this one before, so it'd be really interesting to see what we think of this one. Not too sweet at all. I actually think it could be a little bit sweeter. The flavors do come through quite nice, quite light and refreshing. So now I'm gonna try this one, and this one obviously was the Franklin London and Sons. We're gonna see how that one goes. So cheers. There's a little bit more fizz to it, and I'm someone who doesn't drink a lot of fizz, so I quite I much prefer that one feels a bit lighter. This one, it almost tastes a bit blander. So the time has come, drum roll, where I reveal my favorite which to be honest, I think you might have an inkling at this point, but I think my favorite has to be the Double Dutch cucumber and watermelon. I think it was just a more the flavors came together so much nicer, and it was so light and refreshing. It went so well with the kangaroo. It was a five out of five for me. Unfortunately, this one ate too much fizz, unfortunately. The flavor was quite bland, and it's not something I would pick up again, unfortunately. So it has to be the Double Dutch for me. Thank you so much. I need to now enjoy the rest of my gin. As you can see, there is still some left to enjoy, and I'm definitely gonna be picking this one up again in the shops because it is such a good flavorsome tonic that works beautifully with gin. So thank you so much.
transcriptUserEnriched.summary: In this video review, Debbie from Watch Humans tries two different tonics with a Scottish gin: Double Dutch cucumber and watermelon, and Franklin London and Sons elderflower and cucumber. She finds both tonics visually appealing but prefers the Double Dutch for its light and refreshing flavor, rating it a five out of five. In contrast, she finds the Franklin tonic bland and too fizzy, stating she would not choose it again. Overall, she expresses enthusiasm for the Double Dutch tonic and plans to purchase it again.

I'm gonna try and make a little concoction because I don't really like lemonade that much. So I have my glass. I've got a slice of lime in it and some ice. I'm gonna pour the lemonade in. It's meant to be used as like oh, no. It's like a mixer. I don't like gin or much alcohol, so I'm gonna try a little mocktail. Gonna put some lime juice in it. Just having a of underlook. Some lime juice, and I wanna try with a bit of grenadine syrup for a bit of color and for a bit of fun flavor. Just a little bit. I'm gonna give that a try rather than just plain lemonade. It's okay. It's okay. Maybe a bit too much lime juice in it, but I'm not the biggest lemonade fan. So for me, it's fine because for me, it's more like a kind of a lime soda. It's more like a bit of a soda watery because it's not very strong, and I like that. But I'm not, like, a big lemonade fan. So it's a cool bottle, though. Would I have it in, a cocktail, like, a a gin and tonic lemonade? Probably. Would I buy it? Not? Not really. Nothing wrong with it. I'm just not a big lemonade girl, but it's not very lemon. It's quite tame, like soda water, hint of lemon. So probably good for a mixer. But yeah.

They acknowledge that while the drink is fine and could work as a mixer, they personally would not purchase it due to their preference against lemonade. Overall, they appreciate the bottle design but express that the flavor is not strong enough for their taste.

transcriptUserEnriched.transcriptText: Everyone. My name is Debbie, and today on watch humans, I'm back with some beautiful tonic to try with some beautiful gin. So first up, we've got the Double Dutch lemonade as you can see in this beautiful bottle, and then we've got the Schweppes lemonade in the can here. So first impressions, obviously, this one has a more elegant and beautiful and eye catching bottle. However, Schweppes is a well known brand and something that I would be drawn to because I know the brand and I know the lemonade tastes really, really tasty. So both really good for different reasons. I obviously haven't tried the Double Dutch yet, and I'm really intrigued to see how that one goes. I've already got some beautiful ice as you can see here into identical glasses. So one ice cube in both and now we're gonna add the same gin as we did last time so we can compare the two tonics and really get a good idea of which one we think is the best. Again, it's the Cairngorm gin which is a beautiful Scottish gin And I really enjoy it, and it tends to go quite well with a lot of tonics. So I'm gonna add a splash of this. Again, not huge amount because we've got two drinks again. So we're gonna add this one in. I'm gonna pour that in. So I'm gonna try the Double Dutch one first. So cheers. That lemonade comes through really nicely. It's light and refreshing. It's beautiful. It tastes like a quality drink. Next one. So to be almost a little bit bland, which is a real shame. I have to say the sweetness come through and it and it is really quite nice and sweet. That's something that maybe that one isn't as much, but it isn't as refreshing and flavorsome as I thought it would be, which is quite surprising. So for me, I just think this one has more flavor. It's more light, refreshing. It just seems to go perfectly with that gin. That one I could quite happily drink again and again. It almost just complements it so good. And this one was actually a bit of a shock because I almost expected this one to be the best one because I've had this before and it normally tastes so good, but the sweetness definitely was there. I have to say I didn't have much of the sweetness there. Maybe a little bit of a sweetness to that one would have been nicer. But in terms of the flavor and the fizz, this would just lack that a little bit. It almost tastes a bit bland in comparison, and it didn't seem to go as well with the gin, which is a real surprise. But for me, the winner today has to be the Double Dutch. So thank you so much.
transcriptUserEnriched.summary: In this video review, Debbie compares two lemonades: Double Dutch and Schweppes. She finds the Double Dutch lemonade to be light, refreshing, and flavorful, making it a perfect complement to the Cairngorm gin. In contrast, she describes the Schweppes lemonade as somewhat bland and lacking in flavor, despite its sweetness. Ultimately, she declares Double Dutch the winner of the taste test.

transcriptUserEnriched.transcriptText: I'm making an alcohol free PIM style drink using vinegar, specifically balsamic vinegar. So I put in my cucumber, my strawberries, and my mint, and I'm just putting in a tiny bit of this just as if you would as if you're putting in a Pimm's drink. So just a little tiny bit at the bottom. It's gone all over the mint. That should be fine. And then I'm gonna be trying these two different lemonades. So I've got a Schweppes one in a can, and then I've got a Double Dutch one. So let's give them a pour. I know this sounds odd, but trust me, it sounds just it sounds it tastes just like Pins. Amazing. So obviously, there's not as much in the can. So I'll just fill this up to the same level so that we're completely equal. There we are. So this on my right is the sweeps, and then my left is the Double Dutch. So they both look about the same, don't they? Let's give it a go. That's really nice. Honestly, it tastes just like a pimps. I wish you could have a try. It's really good. So sweeps, Double Dutch. Interestingly, so the Double Dutch is not quite as sweet as the sweeps. Put my teeth in. It's a hard one to choose. That's a bit too sweet for me actually, the sweats, but I wouldn't have known that unless I'd also had a try on another one. So if I had to choose, I'd say that the Double Dutch would be the one for me.

transcriptUserEnriched.transcriptText: Hi, guys. It's Louise. I'm trying the Fix8 Water Kefir Peach and Mango Flavored Live Sparkling Soda. I don't really know what to think about a sparkling soda that's flavored. I mean, I've never tried one. Just normal, like, soda with vodka lime when you go out. But let's give this one a taste. That's actually not as sodary as I was expecting, is a good thing because soda isn't my favorite. And I'm also really glad that it's more mango than peach. It's really nice. I could drink that all day. I think this is something that I could recommend to anyone. It's such nice weather today, so I thought I'd come and enjoy this on my balcony. And I'm gonna sit here for the rest of the afternoon and drink this. Have a look at a little bit more. So Juicy Peach meets Lush Mango, definitely agree with that. Skipping out early and diving into summer. Well, I have left work early today, and it feels like summer. So this is the perfect drink to have. Cheers. Some of my other favorite things about it is that it's bursting with live cultures, great for gut health, and it's really low in calories. Let's have a look at how many there are. So there's about 60 calories in this whole can, and it's better than peach iced tea. Much yummier, and it's a super, super summery drink.
transcriptUserEnriched.summary: Louise reviews the Fix8 Water Kefir Peach and Mango Flavored Live Sparkling Soda, expressing surprise at its taste, which is less soda-like than expected. She appreciates the stronger mango flavor over peach, finds it refreshing for summer, and notes its health benefits, including low calories and live cultures. Overall, she enjoys the drink and recommends it to others.

transcriptUserEnriched.transcriptText: Good morning. I'm Leni, and I am a kombucha skeptic, but I'm here this morning to try this fixate kombucha ginger and turmeric. I had a very long day yesterday. Very early start this morning. I'm ready for work now, but instead of my morning cup of tea, I'm gonna try this and see if it perks me up and keeps me going for the day. I know ginger and turmeric's meant to be really good for you, so I'm hoping. But, also, I don't know what it's about kombucha, but something just never made me really like it. So let's see if this can change my mind. That it tastes a bit like ginger ale. Ginger definitely comes through, not so much the turmeric, but it does have 25% of your vitamin c. It's low calories. It's got live cultures, and it's just gonna help your butt your butt, sorry, gut and bacteria. And it's vegan as well, plant based. Do you know what? I think I'll drink that. Yeah. Fixate kombucha. You might have switched me.
transcriptUserEnriched.summary: Leni, a kombucha skeptic, tries Fixate Kombucha with ginger and turmeric as an alternative to her morning tea. She notes that it tastes somewhat like ginger ale, highlighting the ginger flavor and the drink's health benefits, including vitamin C, low calories, live cultures, and being vegan. Ultimately, she expresses a positive change in her perception of kombucha, indicating she might continue to drink it.

transcriptUserEnriched.transcriptText: Hi. I'm Luna. Right, guys. I'm gonna let you in on a secret. Never tried kombucha before, so I don't know what to expect. All I know is that it's supposed to be good for your gut health. I try the fixate version first of all. Here it goes. That's quite refreshing actually. It's got a little mild sparkle to it and can definitely taste the basil and the strawberry together. It's a good combination actually. Here goes this one. Oh, that's loads fizzier than that one. It reminds me more of a pop though this, which feels a little bit more, you know, familiar. Which one do I prefer? I think maybe this one for familiarity, but this one for taste. But overall, probably this one because it's got added vitamin c.
transcriptUserEnriched.summary: Luna tries two versions of kombucha for the first time, noting the refreshing taste and mild sparkle of the first version, which has a good basil and strawberry combination. The second version is fizzier and reminds her of soda, making it feel more familiar. She prefers the second version for familiarity but appreciates the taste of the first, ultimately favoring the first version due to its added vitamin C.

transcriptUserEnriched.transcriptText: Hello. My name is Lana. I am just currently enjoying the sun as I work from home. I'm 30. I live in London. I work in hospitality sector. And today, I'm trying this Fix8 water kefir, which I'm really excited for. I really don't only have a cold which is buggy because it's sunny. I shouldn't have a cold, sunny. So I'm kinda trying to do anything I can to help with that. I am big into kombucha. I'm big into kefir. I'm big into gut health. I've actually never had a water kefir. I've had obviously the milk version which I do like but I sometimes struggle with the taste and I don't want to get those really artificial ones. I tend to go for the natural one but then I find it a bit sometimes it's a bit salty for me. I have a lot of kombucha. I guess they're a bit more kind of palatable especially not the fruit ones So I try and have one of those like every other day because I really I'm a big believer in like gut health and mental health and that link. So yeah. I'm really excited to try this because I've got track of water one. I like the branding. It's super funky and cool. I like that it's got no added sugar, I guess. I mean, that's not a huge thing for me, but I think it would be good for me, like, in the afternoon not to have, like, a diet coke. I'd swap that out. Also, women built, we love love love love. And it sort of matches my nails. So if you're looking for a nail model, hit me up. First impressions, I don't really like the smell, I'm not gonna lie, but I think it'll taste different. But maybe because I'm just used to like super sugary flavored drinks. I'm not usually that sort of like fermented smell, I guess. Oh, I'm not sure how I feel about that. I'm kind of torn because on one hand I'm like, I know the fermented stuff tastes different to your normal sugary pop. But on the other hand, it is quite a bitter taste. But then I do like it. It's no. It's growing on me. It's growing on me. Differentiation between the cat and so it's like super bright and like, I guess sugary for lack of a better word. And the flavor, I guess I expected it to be a lot more sweet, but maybe that's just on me. Maybe a bit more explaining about like what to expect from the flavor because currently it's quite like high energy and this and that. It is growing on me. I'm not sure if I like this flavor but I would have it because I know it probably brings me health benefits so it's a bit confusing so it's probably not the best review. But I would definitely have this in place of like an Olipop or a different soda that we have like a dash water for example because I just think this is better. You'll get help which I'm big believer in. So I'd say mixed reviews but I would have it because of health benefits. Thanks.
transcriptUserEnriched.summary: Lana, a 30-year-old working from home in London, reviews Fix8 water kefir while battling a cold. She is enthusiastic about gut health and has experience with kombucha and kefir. Although she finds the smell of the water kefir off-putting and initially struggles with its bitter taste, she acknowledges its potential health benefits and expresses a willingness to incorporate it into her diet instead of sugary sodas. Overall, her review is mixed, highlighting both her initial hesitation and eventual acceptance of the product.

transcriptUserEnriched.transcriptText: Buttercup here. Hope everyone's enjoying this lovely hot weather. So I'm gonna be comparing the taste of this lovely fixate kombucha with ginger and turmeric with another one that I buy all the time, which is the one living kombucha with ginger and turmeric. Now interestingly, this one usually comes in a can. I couldn't find this one in a can, but actually, I couldn't find any other brands apart from the fixate. And I've been to like three or four different shops. Interesting. Anyway, so it's brewed with completely natural ingredients. It's good for immune and gut health. Let's give it a go. Really lovely. Really lovely taste. Really sharp, but not too sharp. And now for the one living, which also makes the same sort of claim. So it's organic with live cultures and it's authentically brewed. So let's try that one. Tastes very similar. I'm not sure if this one is sparkling or not actually. Not sure. Let me just pour it out. We'll have a go. I'll be back in a second. Why can you never find a clean glass when you want one? So this is the fixate. So we can see that sparkling, got a lovely fizz. And this is the one living. That has got a fizz, but not as fizzy, I would say. So you can see the color very similar, but the Fixate is slightly paler than the One Living. Give it one last go. Six eight. One Living. Really hard to choose between them. I'd say it's a draw. I think the taste and their sparkle is really kind of comparable, and it's quite hard to tell the difference.
transcriptUserEnriched.summary: Buttercup compares two kombucha brands: Fixate and One Living, both with ginger and turmeric. She notes that Fixate is brewed with natural ingredients and is good for immune and gut health. After tasting both, she finds them to have similar flavors, with Fixate being slightly paler and fizzier. Ultimately, she concludes that it's a draw between the two, as their taste and sparkle are comparable.

transcriptUserEnriched.transcriptText: Hi there. Let's make the easiest strawberry mocktail, just three ingredients. Start with plenty of ice. I love loading it up because drinks taste so much better this way. Next, add some fresh strawberries from the juicy fruity cake. And now pour in a chilled fix eight strawberry kombucha. Look at the color, so vibrant. It's very light, easy, and refreshing. Also, it has way fewer calories, so no need to worry about the nasties. Such drink is perfect for a warm day, a party with friends, or even a summer barbecue.

transcriptUserEnriched.transcriptText: So I've been asked to try this antioxidant fix8 kombucha for ginger and turmeric knife and soda. First of all, I just want to explain, I've recently had a hysterectomy and some other procedures done in my stomach, and my, my gut is really still suffering, so I've saved this to try now. I think my ginger and turmeric is gonna help me. Obviously, the Kombucha has the oxidants in it, it protects against cellular damage doesn't it? Proves your gut health, and aids in inflammatory. This particular one has, I'll show you, 5% vitamin C in it 38 calories. It's also made in The UK so that's good. It's plant based which I'm a vegetarian but then most of these sort of drinks are and it's a fully recyclable can so there's nothing really to say I'm positive about this. Anything will be the taste. I'm gonna try some. Let's see what we make it. Do you know what? I'm gonna be honest with you. I've never had some butcher before in my life. Not the sort of thing I normally buy, but since all my health problems and that now I'm not going for all of this sort of stuff. So let's try. I don't know. I don't know. It's not as strong tasting as I thought. It's quite a weak taste. It's very fizzy. It's drinkable. I'd prefer the ginger to be a little stronger. Maybe the turmeric brings that down. Turmeric, I don't know. It's not bad, you know. I'm not sure if I'm gonna go out and buy it again, but due to my health problems I'm certainly going to finish this can. And if I can find a reasonably enough price, I probably will, just to help with my immune health and get my stomach up back up and going again. Eight out of 10 for me. No pun required because it's the Fix8, but thank you for letting me try this.

They appreciate its plant-based and recyclable qualities but find the taste weak and prefer a stronger ginger flavor. Despite this, they express willingness to finish the drink and possibly repurchase if priced reasonably, rating it an 8 out of 10.

transcriptUserEnriched.transcriptText: So today I went to my local Sainsbury's to get a meal deal, and I decided to try the fixate kombucha. The two options were Sicilian citrus and ginger turmeric. And I had this with some fridge raiders and a Caesar salad. I think this is such a good meal deal plan. As for the drink itself, I really love the packaging and the taste was honestly super refreshing especially for a hot day. It's low calories and helps with your immunity which I absolutely love. I had a long day ahead of me so this was perfect. I would rate this a four out of five.

transcriptUserEnriched.transcriptText: Hello. I just got back from a trip to Barcelona. I ate absolutely everything in sight. And so needless to say, my gut is kind of on ropes. I am not known for having a super great functioning gut, so today I'm super excited to try some kombucha. This is the fix eight kombucha in the flavor strawberry basil. This drink is 25% of your daily vitamin c intake, which is fantastic because, I mean, I'm always getting sick. It's low in calories. It's plant based, so it's vegan friendly, and it also contains live cultures that are scientifically backed for release in the gut. So I'm very excited. The drink is made in The UK, and its cans are infinitely recyclable. It's also a female owned company. Anyway, this is what it looks like. The packaging is super cute. I have no qualms with the packaging. Let's give this a go. Smells really good. I love strawberry flavored things. It's, like, my favorite flavor ever, but I've never tried anything that's, like, strawberry basil. Let's give it a go. That's really good. The taste isn't super sweet, which I appreciate because I don't think I would enjoy kombucha if it was super sweet. You can definitely taste the strawberry. The basil's a little light, but I think I like that. I don't know if I would enjoy this more if it tasted more like basil. Yeah. I'm a fan. I'm definitely a fan. This might be a weird thing to say, but I enjoy that it's not too carbonated. I guess super carbonated drinks also upset my stomach. I like the fact that it's, like, a little bit sparkling, but it's, like, not carbonated. I honestly would rate this a 4.5 out of five stars. It's a super light flavor, which I really enjoy. You can definitely taste the strawberry and a little bit of the basil, but it's not too basily. All around, really great drinks. Super good study drink, honestly, because I am someone who runs through drinks when I study. And if I'm doing a twelve hour a day, I need a lot of drinks. So I'm definitely adding this to my lineup. Mhmm. Yeah. I'll see you guys later.

The reviewer enjoys the taste, noting it's not overly sweet or carbonated, and rates it 4.5 out of 5 stars, considering it a great drink for studying. They also mention the appealing packaging and the fact that it's from a female-owned company.

transcriptUserEnriched.transcriptText: This is my review of the Fix8 Kombucha in the flavor Sicilian Citrus. Today, I'm working from home, and I'm gonna be trying the Fix8 Kombucha in the flavor Sicilian Citrus. Really looking forward to drinking this because I absolutely love Kombucha. A lot of the time I find that Kombucha tastes really weird, but this does just kind of taste like lemonade. It has a little bit of a hint of that weird kombucha flavor but it's not too strong, which I really do like. I'm going to continue to drink this now as I go through a meeting. I'll update you after I have finished the drink. So I finished my meeting not long ago and I have finished the drink, as you can see. I will say I really did like the flavor. It didn't taste too strong of, like, the standard Kombucha flavor that you sometimes get. It was really easy to drink. And it was also reading the back of the can and I like that it's good for your immune health, obviously because there are live cultures in there. It's also low in calorie, which is good, and it's also vegan friendly. I'm not personally vegan, but you know, I can still appreciate that it is plant based, so it can it can be enjoyed by more people. I was also reading the back of it and there's not very many ingredients in it, which is a really good thing. And I do recognize all of the ingredients that have been included, so that means I know what I'm putting into my body and that makes me quite happy. As far as Kombuchas go, I think this is a really good one. And if I was looking for a Kombucha in the shop, I probably would pick this one up again because I know it's good for me and the flavor is quite good as well. So overall, I'm gonna rate this a five out of five. I really did enjoy this one and I'll be really excited to try any other flavors.

The reviewer rates it a perfect five out of five and expresses interest in trying other flavors.

transcriptUserEnriched.transcriptText: It's mid morning, and I'm working from home. So I'm just gonna grab myself a drink. I'm gonna try out the Fix8 New Kefir drinks. These are all natural. They've got nothing in here that is bad for you. They are fermented drinks, they're made with just all natural fruit. I'm gonna try out the peach and mango. So it's just mid morning, I'm having this as a drink. I would normally go for like a kombucha drink or something like that, but these are new, I wanted to try them out. I have things like Kefir yogurt in my usual routine anyway. So with this being a drink, I thought it was sometimes a little bit easier to incorporate into my day whilst I'm on the go. So I thought I'd give these a try. This peach and mango sounds absolutely delicious, so let's open it up. It smells so good. That is amazing. The flavor in that is so strong. It's so good. And it's it's got a really lovely strong flavor. Almost like sweet as well, and like it should be full of sugar, but it genuinely isn't full of sugar. It's got no added sugar and low calories, but trying that you wouldn't think it. Oh, that is delicious. That I think is gonna be one of my new favorite go to drinks. I definitely recommend this to someone who drinks something like this already or already has Kefir, but wants like an alternative to it because these are amazing.

They appreciate that the drink is all-natural, low in calories, and has no added sugar. The reviewer finds the flavor to be strong and delicious, expressing enthusiasm for the drink and recommending it to others who enjoy similar beverages.

transcriptUserEnriched.transcriptText: Time for battle of the booches. I'm a big kombucha drinker, but let's see how this one compares with this one. I'm going to be rating on flavor, fizz, sweetness, aftertaste, and of course, gut check. Today's lineup, we've got the fix eight strawberry and basil kombucha. It's got live cultures which will help your immune health. It's low in calories, and it describes itself as a live sparkling soda. It also has 25% of your daily vitamin c. Magic. And we're comparing that with the remedy wild berry kombucha. No sugar, busy and refreshing apparently, delicately brewed for thirty days, raw vegan, unpasteurized, and best served chilled. So here we go. Otherwise, looking very similar. But how will they taste? Grenadine Wildberry to start with. The flavor comes through nicely. It tastes like kombucha, but it doesn't taste too vinegary. So if you're not a hardcore kombucha person, you'd probably still like it. I mean, it tastes like berries, a little bit syrupy, so quite sweet. A slight aftertaste. I wonder if there's sweetener in it. Yes. So it's got stevia in it. It's got a slight aftertaste of stevia, but I'm really sensitive to that. Oh, it's got hibiscus tea and I thought there was a slight floral note, but I'm never expert. I'd say it's more sweet than sharp. Time to try fixate strawberry and basil, see how it will compare. Oh, that's really unique. Strong flavor of strawberries, but slightly mellowed with, I'm guessing, the basil. It kind of tastes like those strawberry flavor of a hard boiled sweet. And I'm not getting any aftertaste. Ah, no sweeteners in it. It's interesting. In terms of flavor, I think initially, I prefer this one less syrupy or acidic y berry. However, the stevia aftertaste is not the one for me. This one, initially, I don't like the taste quite as much because it's a little bit sweeter as opposed to sharp. However, like, it's not leaving any stevia aftertaste. This one is more consistently sweet, whereas this one's zingy, and then it's got that stevia taste. I think if I were to choose to buy another one again, it's a tough call. But I think overall, I picked fixate just because I really can't stand Stevia aftertaste. Stevia kind of upsets my stomach as well, so I'll probably be bloated if I drink all this one. Strawberry and the uniqueness of the bathwater is so good. So in the battle of the boojes, my winner is six eight.
transcriptUserEnriched.summary: The video review compares two kombucha drinks: Fixate Strawberry and Basil and Remedy Wild Berry. The reviewer evaluates them based on flavor, fizz, sweetness, aftertaste, and gut health benefits. Remedy Wild Berry is noted for its berry flavor and sweetness but has a stevia aftertaste that the reviewer dislikes. Fixate Strawberry and Basil is praised for its unique flavor without any aftertaste, leading the reviewer to prefer it overall despite its initial sweetness. The reviewer ultimately declares Fixate as the winner due to the absence of the stevia aftertaste, which they find unpleasant.

transcriptUserEnriched.transcriptText: Hello. I just got back from my morning run, and now I just kinda wanna wind down, relax, and get on with some admin and uni work. And I'm deciding to have this little guy here. It's the Skip CBD sparkling water drink in the flavor lemon and basil. I think packaging wise, it's super, super cute. I love the minimalistic look, and the logo is so cute. And the little lemon and basil graphic, Really sweet. Ingredients wise, it's got no added sugar. There's five milligrams of cold pressed active CBD, and it's also vegan. A lot of natural flavors in this. And it's nice and chilled to cool me off. And CBD will help relax me and kind of mellow my brain out. So let's give it a go. So satisfying. Oh, you can really smell the basil. I love the smell of basil, so that's a good sign. That is so refreshing. Yeah. I needed this. This is lovely. Definitely more hints of basil than lemon. I don't really taste lemon, but it's good. Not a huge fan of sparkling water. I prefer kind of still stuff. But, no, this is fantastic. I would buy a truckload of these. Yeah. Very refreshing. Highly recommend.

Despite not being a fan of sparkling water, they express a strong liking for this drink and highly recommend it.

transcriptUserEnriched.transcriptText: We all know how life can get and it can be stressful at the best of times. So I'm always really interested about products that have CBD in them. Now a little bit, I don't know, not sure about it. So here's a product that I've I've got to try. It's called Skip. This is the Elderflower Mint flavor. It's cold pressed. It's got active CBD in it, zero grams of added sugar. So I'm gonna give it a try. It's vegan as well. I'm not vegan, but I think it's always good. The more products we have out there that are the smell is interesting. Not too sure about it. It's quite herbal, kind of what's the kinda garden y. Yeah. It's definitely got the elderflower coming through. Definitely got the like more of like a wild mint as well. That's what it looks like. I'm not sure if it's my kind of vibe, but it's definitely kind of giving a summer barbecue in the garden kind of vibe. It's giving like a a Pimm's kind of flavor. I don't know how to describe it. It's really interesting, really very different to anything else that I've had recently. I wouldn't say it's personally for me, but I'd say if that's more your vibe and you like that kind of thing, then this is for you. And I can see that it could be really good for, like, helping you calm down in the evening or just a little moment of bliss, you know, throughout the day. So yeah.

They acknowledge its potential calming effects and suggest it may appeal to those who enjoy such flavors.

transcriptUserEnriched.transcriptText: Peach and ginger Skip, the true calm within. It's made in UK. It has no other sugar. It's vegan friendly and gluten free. It contains five milligrams of cold pressed CBD. Skip as a company is using eco friendly packaging, is trying to reduce the environmental impact. Consuming CBD gives lots of benefits to the body and the mind. Now today, I'm comparing Skip to Tripp, which is its competitor. And I must say, out of all the things which I read, I feel very not at ease that Tripp is still using the old foundation from Food Standard Agency about daily limit of consumption CBD, which used to be seventy milligrams. But since October 2023, it got lower to ten milligrams. Now in terms of the drinks, they both have the kick of the ginger, and personally, I prefer the skip over trip. That would be a four out of five stars for me.

transcriptUserEnriched.transcriptText: Hiya, my name is Valerie. Today I'm going to be reviewing the Skip Elderflower and Mint Cold Pressed Active CBD Sparkling Drink. It's zero added sugar, five milligrams of active CBD and it's vegan. I've been sent this to review from Watch Humans Try It. I'm a big fan of CBD drinks. I've tried a couple of other brands and a couple of other flavors. I have tried as well a different flavor in Skip, and I really liked that. So I'm hoping that this will be really pleasant as well. That's lovely. That's really nice. That's really refreshing and really minty. There is there's the same there's the same flavor, elderflower and mint, that's available from Tripp, but their version tastes more like mint, the sweet. It tastes like polos. This one tastes really like fresh mint. It tastes really strongly of, yeah, like that fresh fresh mint herb as you would get it in, like, cocktail or a mocktail. So one of the main things I like about CBD drinks is I like to have them as an alcohol alternative, so that I think would be a really good replacement if you're someone that likes, mojitos or that sort of fresh herby type of cocktail but you don't want to drink. That's that would be a really good replacement. That's really nice. It definitely tastes more like mint than it tastes like eldflower, but I think that's normal, that sort of mixture. It's really lightly sparkling. So yeah, I'm really pleased with that. Yeah. That's really delicious. Good job, Skip.

Overall, she expresses satisfaction with the product, indicating a positive experience.

transcriptUserEnriched.transcriptText: Hey, guys. I'm at work right now, and it's honestly been quite a stressful day. So I figured it's the best time for me to review Skip's CBD Drink in the flavor Elderflower Mint, which is my favorite. So this is a sparkling cold pressed active CBD drink. I typically love anything with mint in it. The one thing I'm scared about is whether the CBD flavor will be too strong as a single can contains five milligrams of cold pressed CBD. But let's see. Cheers. That's really good. Okay. The elderflower and mint flavor is really strong, which I love. Cause if that's what it writes on the can, then that's the flavor I want to be getting. And I actually don't taste any of the weird herbally CBD tastes that I typically dislike. Each can has zero added sugar, and it's completely vegan. And what's really interesting about Skip, a CBD brand, is that Skip CBD is eight times the strength of standard CBD isolate. Hopefully, it relaxes me. I really like the packaging. I love the slim can, and this can is also a 100% recyclable. I think the flavor is definitely there. It's completely accurate to what they market. I love the can. Love that it's recyclable. Although, I would say the website should definitely have a bit more on the mission and the impact and maybe some education about what active CBD means because as a consumer I don't really know the difference between an inactive and active. However, the taste is definitely there and I do love that I can get such a convenient can of high quality CBD whenever I'm stressed. So in all, my really good score would be 4.5 out of five.
transcriptUserEnriched.summary: The reviewer discusses their experience with Skip's CBD Drink in Elderflower Mint flavor, expressing satisfaction with its strong flavor and lack of typical CBD taste. They appreciate the drink's vegan and sugar-free qualities, as well as its recyclable packaging. The reviewer notes the drink's high CBD strength compared to standard products and mentions a desire for more information on the brand's website regarding CBD types. Overall, they rate the drink 4.5 out of 5.

transcriptUserEnriched.transcriptText: Hi. I'm Jenny. Today, I'm going to be trying the Skip CBD Peach Ginger Drink. I'm about to have some friends over, and normally, I'd have a glass of wine, but today I thought I'd go alcohol free. So I'm going to be making a little mocktail situation. Press sip. Oh, wow. Yeah. That's super gingery. I love ginger beer personally. I'd say it almost reminds me of that, but if you're more looking for a peach drink, this isn't quite for you. It really is nice, though. It feels like quite not overpoweringly sweet. I it's obviously a bit too early to tell what the CBD does, but it does say triple strength on the can, so I have high hopes. I also really like that it's all natural. It's from stevia extract. I don't feel like I'm getting any, you know, artificial sugars or anything. So, yes, high hopes. I'll check back in and let you know. Okay. So I finished the drink, and I'm feeling quite relaxed. Like, I've had a glass of wine, but without any of the, obviously, like, the bad effects of alcohol. So just really chill, and I'm actually gonna change it from a three to a four stars. Really impressed with this product.

transcriptUserEnriched.transcriptText: Hi, guys. I've literally finished an interview, and I was very, very tired. And the bell rang, and I received a parcel.
