It says here that it is vegan friendly as well, so the drink offered a sophisticated wellness boost as well. I would suggest that it's really nice. The cold press method has really locked in the pure and natural flavors, and the light bubbles add a lively touch to it. Perfect for unwinding or just enjoying a clean refreshing sip. Really good for the post workout.

transcriptUserEnriched.transcriptText: I've got the Skip True Calm drink today. This is a cold pressed active CBD. Now I've never actually tried a CBD, so little apprehensive about it. I've heard a lot about, you know, what it could be and what it couldn't be. Some good, some not so good, but today is my chance to form my own opinion about CBD. This is a Lemon Basil Sparkling CBD Drink, and it has got fruit juices, botanicals, along with cold pressed CBD. So let's give that a try. Love that sound. Oh, that's strong. I can definitely taste the basil, not so much lemon. It's kind of quite a nice drink. It's very I don't know how to say this, but it's kind of like a savory drink, if you know what I mean. So very taste and texture like Coca Cola or Pepsi, whatever the the sparkling flavor, the smoothness, and everything, but it is savory. It's it's not sweet at all. It's nice, really refreshing. I like it. Think it could also go really well if you add fruit juice, if you wanted to drink it a little sweeter, maybe make it stronger with a little alcohol. But it is nice. I like it.

They suggest that it could be enhanced with fruit juice or alcohol but overall express a positive opinion about the drink.

transcriptUserEnriched.transcriptText: Hey, guys. I just got a week's worth of this really interesting looking Uni drink. I've got both the Uplift and the unwind flavor. So come with me for the next week where I try out both of them throughout my ups and my downs, and I find out if they are actually right for me and if they're right for you too. I tried the Uplift drink first, and the immediate thing that jumped out was that the flavor was a whole lot more subtle than I was expecting. I'm used to every now and then having one of those CBD sodas that you can buy, and the flavors in those are usually a whole lot stronger. So this was kind of a nice change. So I tried the Uplift one a few times throughout the week. It's got a great flavor. You can tell that it's made with real ingredients. Sometimes I had Zoom meetings at night, so it was good to have one of the Uplift drinks around about that time when I was losing energy, kind of getting ready to go to bed, but having to stumble into a meeting anyway. And I I did feel kind of a tangible difference after having these drinks as opposed to when I didn't. In a normal week, when I'm looking to find something to drink when I'm getting through work or running errands, I would normally reach for a coffee, and I quite like having a coffee. So I wouldn't want to ever replace that with this. But I think an interesting use case is actually having this drink for later night meetings. The unwind was super nice as well. I think of the two, this was maybe my favorite flavor, the lemon and lavender. It was also a whole lot easier to integrate into my routine. I've gotten into the habit of some nights having one of those wind down CBD drinks, and so this was nice to just swap in for those. It's kind of a nice replacement of having, like, a glass of wine with dinner. It was good to be able to intentionally switch off from work mode and then, I guess, unwind with one of these drinks as just a nice way to end the day. So I think I'll keep drinking the unwind, and I'm interested to see if there is a place before the uplift because I think the effects of the uplift were maybe more noticeable than the unwind. It's just there's less time that I have where I need that. So I can definitely recommend Oonae's drinks to others, especially if you're looking for something a little bit healthier that's gonna make a tangible difference in your day.
transcriptUserEnriched.summary: The reviewer shares their experience with two flavors of Uni drinks, Uplift and Unwind, over a week. They found Uplift to have a subtle flavor and beneficial effects during late-night work meetings, though they still prefer coffee. Unwind, with a lemon and lavender flavor, was easier to integrate into their routine and served as a nice alternative to wine for relaxation. The reviewer recommends both drinks for those seeking healthier options that positively impact their daily life.

challenge.title: Unai Uplift vs Unwind Daily Challenge

transcriptUserEnriched.transcriptText: I've got four assessments to do this week. I'm an independent best interest assess I need to try and get as much work done today as possible. I'm gonna try out this drink by Unai, which is an uplift drink, which is designed to help you to focus and to just give you a little bit of a lift. It's in pink grapefruit and strawberry flavor, and in the drink, it's vegan, and it's got no artificial additives or preservatives. There's lion manes in it, cordyceps, green tea, rhubarb. The cans are textured and I absolutely love the design of the cans. It's a really good drink, I absolutely love the flavour of that. I'm three assessments down and I've just got one left to do now so I'm really pleased with myself. Drinks which is the Calm and Balanced unwind drink. This is lemon and lavender flavor and there's reishi mushrooms in there, ashwagandha, magnesium. You reduce your anxiety and stress levels. It's quite a unique flavor. Again, I love the can. I dread doing maths homework. I absolutely hate it. So I'm gonna see if Unai will help me with that one. I'm gonna do the Uplift. So glad the homework session's over, so I'm gonna unwind with the lemon and lavender, Unai. I do think that the Uplift Grapefruit and Strawberry one is my favorite. It just is a little bit sweeter for me. I definitely had a bit of a lift in the afternoon where I might have had a slump otherwise.
transcriptUserEnriched.summary: The reviewer discusses their experience with two drinks by Unai: the Uplift drink (pink grapefruit and strawberry flavor) and the Calm and Balanced unwind drink (lemon and lavender flavor). They appreciate the design of the cans and the vegan ingredients, which include beneficial elements like lion's mane, cordyceps, and reishi mushrooms. The reviewer finds the Uplift drink effective for focus and energy, especially while completing assessments, and expresses a preference for its sweeter taste. They also mention using the unwind drink to relax after homework, indicating a positive overall experience with both products.

This is very much like during the day, focus, clarity when you need to get stuff done, you need a little bit of a boost. And this is the Unwind Calm and Balance Lemon and Lavender drink. So I'm gonna try these out over the next few days, see how I like them together, see how I like them individually. But today I definitely need a little bit of a boost so I'm going try the pink, grapefruit, and strawberry. And hopefully there's not caffeine in this. It looks like there's a tiny bit of green tea extract but it doesn't look like there's anything concerning for me and my heart problem. So let's give it a taste. It's really nice and fresh. It's not super syrupy but it's definitely not like a flavored water. It has definitely like a flavor to it but quite light. Yeah, tastes like like a kombucha. The Unwind drink helped them relax after a long day, although they find it a bit sweet for nightly use. Overall, they plan to continue trying both drinks individually and together for their intended effects of focus and relaxation.

challenge.title: Unai Uplift vs Unwind Daily Challenge

transcriptUserEnriched.transcriptText: Oh, dear. It's one of those, days, when I'm running out of energy, running and rushing everywhere. I've done the, Mother's Day event at nursery for my child and dropped my kids to see grandma. Went quickly to do grocery weekly grocery shopping that I do, and I quickly had to put whatever needs to go in the fridge in the fridge because I meant to pick them up in ten minutes. So I'm rushing to pick them up. So I've quickly popped that in. I'm out of breath. It's sunny outside. I feel hot. I feel like I need something refreshing because I feel I'm thirsty, but also that lift, uplift, that energy, extra energy that I'm lacking at the moment. It's almost 01:00 and I'm already exhausted. I don't know why but shopping always makes me exhausted. So I'm gonna be trying out this uplift drink from Unai. It, it has good combination of flavors because two of my favorite fruits are combined into this drink, which is the Pink Grapefruit and Strawberry. So I'm excited to try it and hope it tastes as good as the actual fruits. It helps with focus and vitality. It has lion's mane in it, cordyceps and it's only 30 calorie per can. It's better to keep in a fridge so one is nice and cold. It has natural sweetener stevia. I'm not a big fan, but I don't mind it if the fruits are over what do call it? Overtaking taste. Oh. A little bit of the fizz deck. I don't know if you can hear it. So I'm currently in the car, prepping myself for driving and picking up the kids. That's all the goodness from the can. It is bit, what do you call it, damaged from delivery but that doesn't matter for me. Let's give this a go, see how it tastes. It smells amazing. It's actually like a kind of like a flavored water. It doesn't taste horrible. It's vegan friendly, natural, no artificial coloring and flavors which is nice. So it's a healthy drink of uplift. I want to see how well it does. I've never tried anything like this before, any uplift drink because I feel like what they're trying to say it doesn't always work. That it's just like a pitch, if that makes sense. So I'm glad to to try it and see if that actually helps me, gives me that extra energy I need to last the rest of the day with three kids in the house house chores. It tastes nice. Taste wise, five out of five, definitely. It's not too sweet. It's just the right amount of sweetness. I hate sweet drinks like those water sweet flavored water or squashes or anything like that. I'm no no. But this one's actually nice. And to be honest, it's not actually fizzy. It's it's like a little tiny bit of fizz and that's that's about it. Let's put that in cup holder and drink that while I'm getting the pick picking up the kids. The uplift drink definitely gave me more energy, not a lot, but made me feel positive about the day. Did manage to do everything I needed to do cleaning wise and task wise on on Saturday. So that has definitely improved my mood and the energy level, and it tasted great, kept me hydrating. So definitely see a bit of a difference there.

The drink is low in calories, vegan-friendly, and contains natural ingredients. transcriptUserEnriched.transcriptText: About two years ago, I started meditating and I really, really noticed the benefits. I started at five minutes a day, and now I do thirty minutes to an hour just depending on how I'm feeling and what kind of practice that I want to do. I truly believe that the things that you add into your diet, how you go about your day, how your nervous system status really impacts your meditation practice, so I do like to add things in throughout the day that will complement that. But this week, I'm going to be trying out the Unai Lemon and Lavender Unwind Calm and Balance drink, and this is a sparkling drink that contains reishi, ashwagandha, and loads of other great botanicals and nootropics that are great for inflammation, heart health, detoxification, keeping your body calm and relaxed, and supporting your nervous system as well. So I feel like this is the perfect addition to my kind of lifestyle. I've been drinking these for about a week now, and I have them about thirty minutes before my meditation practice. And I wasn't too sure about the flavor at first. I think it was the lavender, but I did start getting used to that as the week went on. I do like to meditate lots of different ways. So here's me in my infrared sauna blanket, and I also like to use my red light therapy panel at the same time. And I didn't notice a huge difference after drinking them, but I did feel a little bit more relaxed and like I kind of got into a flow of meditation state a little bit quicker, which is great.
transcriptUserEnriched.summary: The reviewer shares their two-year journey with meditation, starting from five minutes a day to now practicing for thirty minutes to an hour. They emphasize the importance of diet and lifestyle on meditation effectiveness. They are trying the Unai Lemon and Lavender Unwind Calm and Balance drink, which contains beneficial ingredients like reishi and ashwagandha. After a week of use, they report feeling slightly more relaxed and achieving a quicker meditative state, despite initial uncertainty about the drink's flavor.

transcriptUserEnriched.transcriptText: Okay. I have some social plans tonight, so I thought I would try this out. Now it is meant to help you unwind and feel, like, calm and balanced, and I find when I go to social situations that I can get very on edge. Yeah. So I thought I'd start this today. I'm gonna try it for a week and see how I get on. I'm planning to take this before social plans, meetings, anything sort of that would cause me stress, maybe even before bed. We'll see. And stuff I normally do to calm down is have a cup of tea. So this may be replacing that and also helping me with my caffeine addiction, which I feel like everybody has at the moment. So yeah, come along with me and then we'll see how how it goes. Okay. So what's also really interesting is that it has Reshee in there, seven hundred and fifty milligrams of it. It has ashwagandha. I don't know how to say that, but it has five hundred milligrams of that. And it's only 32 calories per can, which is amazing. It also has magnesium in it, which is really, really good for sleep. So, yeah, I'm I'm really interested to see how this how this goes. That's really nice. You can definitely taste the lavender, and the lemon is quite strong. It kinda tastes a bit like a lemonade. Really nice, very refreshing and not an intense aftertaste which I tend to hate when I go to my hair. I'm in the middle of getting ready. Yeah, so let's see how I get on. So I actually found that I took this the most on my journey to London and back. It was really nice to have this sort of before or after work, and really actually helped me focus, which was great as I'm definitely a massive procrastinator. And yeah, I've been taking this for around a week. I'm gonna do one last video tomorrow after a bath before I go to sleep. And, yeah, very I would definitely get these again. They need to be added to my daily commute as you can see with all these videos. But yeah.

transcriptUserEnriched.transcriptText: Hi, guys. It's Yas here. I'm going to be trying this Unai Uplift drink for a week and just see how I feel about it. So this is in the flavor pink grapefruit and strawberry. As a prework focus, I'm going to be replacing my usual matcha with this drink. Initial smell, it was a very sweet strawberry smell, which I liked. I couldn't really smell the grapefruit, but now the taste actually really surprised me. I could taste a strawberry, which isn't one of my favorite flavors, but you could definitely taste. It kinda tasted grape y instead of grapefruity, but that hint, it was just it was really good. It wasn't too sweet, and it had a really nice aftertaste as well. So I had to attend this study, and I feel like the drink really helped me focus. And the week after, here's just me and the library working. I don't think I got any side effects from this. It didn't feel too bad on my stomach either. I definitely focused more on my work as well. I didn't crash as much as I usually do. And, yeah, this drink has no artificial flavors. It's a 100% natural, and I'll definitely incorporate this into my routine after drinking this after a week.

She enjoys the sweet strawberry aroma and finds the taste surprisingly pleasant, noting it tastes more like grape than grapefruit. The drink helps her focus during study sessions without causing side effects or stomach discomfort. Yas appreciates that it contains no artificial flavors and plans to continue using it in her routine.

transcriptUserEnriched.transcriptText: Trying this Unai Calm and Unwind. Now this is a drink that's supposed to help when you have stressful moments. I really have got quite a few stressful moments coming up. Tonight is one. I'm going to a party where I don't really know anybody, so sometimes I can feel a bit awkward. So I'm quite worried and stressed about that tonight. I've got a few meetings planned this week as well. Training events where I've got some big groups I'm gonna be training to. So got quite a stressful and also a busy week ahead. Now this is Lemon and Lavender flavor. Some of the key ingredients in here includes Ashwannagon, turmeric, magnesium, but also it's got the Reche mushroom as well. Not sure how this flavor, Lemon and Lavender is gonna go down with me, but gonna give it a go. No little Duke smile to it. Has got a bit of a zing to it. Could be the ginger that's in it. But taste wise, pretty smooth. Not as fizzy as I thought it would be, which is a plus point for me. I think I would drink this during the daytime or late afternoon. transcriptUserEnriched.transcriptText: I'm really excited to be trying Unai. The unwind one. The flavor is lemon and lavender. This contains reishi, magnesium, ashwagandha, burdock root, prickly pear, artichoke leaf, and turmeric. So I'm cooking dinner at the minute. I thought it'd be the perfect time to start unwinding for the evening. We take sleep unwinding down in the evenings very seriously. I'm always on board with anything that helps me to switch off in the evening and only 33 calories per can. I really love lavender anything. It tastes so nice. I feel like the taste or smell of lavender just tells my brain that it's time to switch off. It's exactly the kind of drink that I choose to drink in the evening. Normally in the evenings, I drink some form of sleepy tea. It really helps me to wind down. So I'm gonna be replacing that with this. So I'm gonna be trying it this week, and I'm gonna let you know how I get on. I'm home from work. I'm in my pajamas, and I'm cooking dinner. So again, I'm looking to unwind for the day, and I'm gonna be having another one of these to help me kind of switch off and unwind. It's been a couple of days of drinking the Unai unwind drink in the evenings. I've been having them after work when I really wanna switch off for the day. They definitely do make me feel calmer, more chilled out, which is especially great before bed. I'm just enjoying having them as part of my evening ritual. It's now been a week of drinking these post work to de stress, and I love them. I look forward to having them as part of my routine. I love that they actually contain pretty high levels of adaptogens compared to other drinks on the market, and I will definitely be repurchasing these. Yeah. Love them.

The reviewer plans to repurchase the product.

transcriptUserEnriched.transcriptText: Just come out the shower and it's been a really stressful day. My daughter fell ill and my little son just wanted to be played with the whole time. Now my partner's looking after the children while I can have some time to relax. I'm gonna try the Lemon and Lavender Unwind Unai sparkling drink. We're going for the wine glass as well because why not? It actually looks like white wine. It's got a lovely fizz to it. I can smell the lemon. The lavender is very subtle which I really like as well but I don't mind lavender at all so it can be a lot stronger. It doesn't smell as strong as the strawberry and grapefruit. I'm intrigued. Oh yeah, I can smell it more in the glass. It's really again mouth watering. I don't know why that's happening to me with these drinks. That's really nice, really relaxing. It's got a subtle hint of lavender and I can taste the lemon but it's not sharp and bitter. It's kind of sweet and natural. No problems with any of them on the stomach. I'll let you know this evening if I feel a lot calmer and more balanced. There's no weird aftertaste or anything, not flavours that I would imagine going together, but they actually do. What I like about this food supplement drink is that it contains ashwagandha, reishi, vitamin B12 and magnesium and natural sweetener. I do sometimes take ashwagandha supplements and they really do help me feel more relaxed and balanced. Yeah I'm happy that it's in this drink. I'm just chilling in my pyjamas while the kids are in bed. I do feel relaxed but I think it's more because I went in the shower. I don't feel particularly different. I think it could be a little bit of a placebo effect. I just like the way that it pours like a sparkling wine and it actually looks like one and if you have it in a wine glass placebo effect. The first can, the strawberry and grapefruit flavour, it really made me focus more alert and it woke me up and I couldn't believe the effect that that had. The lavender and lime on the other hand, I feel relaxed but I think I just felt relaxed anyway. I think even if I hadn't had a shower, I'd have felt just relaxed this evening. Even though it's been a bit a stressful day, the smell of the strawberry and grapefruit is so much nicer than the smell of the lemon and lavender. Just a shame that both of them don't taste like they smell, they taste more subtle and lighter and a bit like flavored sparkling water. I absolutely love the first one but I do love the second one as well. For all these reasons I'm gonna give them a four out of five.
transcriptUserEnriched.summary: The reviewer discusses their experience with the Lemon and Lavender Unwind Unai sparkling drink after a stressful day. They appreciate the drink's appearance, subtle flavors, and relaxing qualities, noting the presence of beneficial ingredients like ashwagandha and magnesium. While they find the drink pleasant, they express a preference for the strawberry and grapefruit flavor, which they felt had a more significant effect. Overall, they rate the drink a 4 out of 5.

transcriptUserEnriched.transcriptText: Hi, everyone. My name is Luan, and I have been sent these Unii, unwind calm and balanced drinks. I am gonna be trying these for a week, and I am going to be drinking them after I have done, bedtime with my son. I don't know if any other parents out there, but my kid never wants to go to bed. And bedtime is quite an ordeal, And then he goes to bed quite late anyway, and then I go to bed fairly early. So that period of time between when I've managed to get him down for bed and when I'm gonna go to sleep is probably about an hour. And I find it quite hard to, like, chill out and unwind and sort of get, you know, my mind nice and rested and quiet, ready to be able to sleep after what has been probably quite a traumatic evening and bedtime. So I will let you know later on in the week how I get on. Hi, everyone. So I have been drinking the Uni, lemon and lavender unwind, drinks for a week. This is my last can today. I've got a really busy day today, so I'm gonna have this because I'm feeling a little bit stressed this morning. And I have really found that these have been amazing just to help me sort of relax and de stress. In the evenings, I've been having them before bed just to kind of like wind down instead of having a glass of wine. Having one of these instead and I feel a lot less groggy and it still gives me that kind of relax feeling. So they have been great and I've really enjoyed them, and I definitely would carry on drinking them and be interested to try any other flavors. So thank you.

transcriptUserEnriched.transcriptText: Was lucky to get the opportunity to try Unai Pink Grapefruit and Strawberry Uplift and Lemon and Lavender unwind. The Lemon and Lavender contains some key ingredients like Reshima, and bird of wort, all aimed at helping you unwind and relax. The pink grapefruit and strawberry contains lion's mane, cordyceps, green tea, panicked ginseng, and lemon balm, all aimed at uplifting your mood and helping with focus and energy levels. I have tried these types of ingredients before in drop forms and capsules, so I was excited to see how these would taste infused with these type of flavors. I was having a stressful week and really needed something to help me unwind and relax, so it was the perfect timing for me to try the Unai lemon and lavender unwind. I was so surprised with the beautiful blend of flavors with a slight fizz. It was really enjoyable. I didn't notice any instant results, but after about an hour, I did feel less anxious and stressed. I was really surprised also by how it helped me drift off to sleep more peacefully. It was a dull and gloomy day, and I had a lot of work I needed to get through. So I thought it was the perfect opportunity for me to try the Unai Grapefruit and Strawberry Upland. The flavors again were really perfectly balanced, and I was so shocked by how much I enjoyed this drink. For me, I didn't really have much of an energy boost, but it did really help me focus, and I didn't get as distracted as I usually do when trying to concentrate. I really enjoyed both of these drinks, and it did help me unwinding and uplifting my mood when needed. I would 100% purchase these both again.
transcriptUserEnriched.summary: The reviewer tried two drinks from Unai: Pink Grapefruit and Strawberry Uplift, and Lemon and Lavender Unwind. The Lemon and Lavender drink helped the reviewer relax and sleep better, while the Pink Grapefruit and Strawberry drink improved focus but did not provide a significant energy boost. Overall, the reviewer enjoyed both drinks for their flavor and effectiveness in helping with stress and concentration, expressing a strong intent to purchase them again.

transcriptUserEnriched.transcriptText: Hello. So I am on my lunch break. And today, we are going to be trying the Unai. I keep wanting to say like Unai, which is just really funny to me. And this is the Uplift focus on vitality. Now, I'm currently working on a report, and there's a lot of numbers involved. So I thought what better way to uplift my focus on vitality than through this. So I'm really excited to try Pink Grapefruit and Strawberry. It's a really interesting combination. I don't typically like grapefruit, but I like that kind of like blood orange, like sour kind of bitterness. So I'm quite interested to see how this will taste. Also on a similar note, I know this has the lion's manes and cordyceps. I always want to say quadriceps, but cordyceps is it like the the mushrooms. It's vegan, is great, also natural. Again, stevia. But like on the whole, everything else is great and I would say this yeah. Like it seems pretty great. Oh, definitely has that grapefruit. Doesn't really taste like grapefruit though. Definitely smells smells a lot more grapefruity than it tastes, which is really interesting. No. There is a bit of grapefruit, but, like, it has a strawberry aftertaste. That actually slightly reminds me of Kvaerberg's, you know, like the the strawberry kind of I think they do a strawberry lime like flavored cider, but obviously this is non alcoholic. But like it's interesting that has a similar kind of thing, but I guess it's that strawberry flavor. And I think strawberry is quite hard flavor to get artificially, but I think this is good. It's also quite gentle. Yeah. I I like this. I think the only thing they'll put me off would be the the stevia. And but what I do like is there isn't like direct caffeine in it. It's more like l theanine, which is caffeine, but it's like the green tea caffeine. So it's like more of a natural thing. But I probably wouldn't get this as like an energy drink. I mean, it's probably the most natural that you can get of the, like, the energy drinks, but I'm not really someone who typically goes for an energy drink. So I'd probably have to say I would go for a kombucha over this purely because this is like caffeinated in some way. Whereas kombucha I guess actually kombucha is a bit caffeinated too. Okay. Scratch that. But I think for me, the kombucha flavor has like has the edge and also the fermented. Although, this also has fermented. Yeah. But I do like it. I guess it is kind of like a kombucha then. But again, stevia is just the one thing that puts me off because I don't want to be having it regularly because it has the inverse effect on the gut. So all these things are great. Like, I saw the little like the little poster with everything saying how has all these great ingredients which is beautiful. But in my mind stevia just acts as like a complete reset to that. And I don't know biologically whether that actually is the case, but obviously stevia disrupts the good bacteria. And I know there was a lemon and lavender, which I kind of liked, but I like a I don't know, like a ginger and lemon for me. So that would just be like my personal preference.
transcriptUserEnriched.summary: The reviewer tries the Unai Uplift drink focused on vitality, specifically the Pink Grapefruit and Strawberry flavor. They express excitement about the drink, noting its interesting taste profile, which is more strawberry than grapefruit. The drink contains natural ingredients like lion's mane and cordyceps mushrooms, and the reviewer appreciates its vegan and natural aspects. However, they express some concern about the use of stevia, which they believe may negatively impact gut health. Overall, they find the flavor enjoyable but would prefer it less frequently due to the stevia. They rate it a 3 out of 5, indicating a moderate level of satisfaction.

transcriptUserEnriched.transcriptText: Hi, guys. Tonight, I'm gonna be testing the Double Dutch Ginger Beer premium mixer and the Ventamin's classic ginger beer. I'm gonna be adding a rhubarb and ginger liqueur and a slice of lime to each glass as well. So they're gonna be tested as mixers. First, I'm gonna start with the Double Dutch. It's got a nice fizz to it. And then half of that squeeze of lime. Oh, it's very light, very bubbly. I really like the way it fizzes but not for too long and the bubbles just disappear into the drink. It's got a very nice smell of ginger. It smells almost creamy. I quite like it. That is really nice. I love how subtle the flavours are. Then you get that really strong after kick of ginger. I can feel it in the mouth now but it feels really natural and I love the way that the bubbles fizz on your tongue with these Double Dutch mixers. The ginger's still there. It's intense but it's not fiery. It's just a really nice feeling. The intensity fizzes up your nose a little bit, but then it disappears and the lime really makes it juicy and zesty and it's a great drink. Okay. Let's also do the same for the Ventamin ginger beer. So half of the rhubarb and ginger liqueur and this is a screw top. It's a sort of oaky intense smell. It does smell a bit like it's been sat there a while. A squeeze of of lime as before. Plop that in. Slight fears disappears just the same as the Double Dutch. It's very subtle. It's a lot more subtle than the Double Dutch. There's no intensity there. The flavours sort of hit the back of your throat when you swallow but it's less intense. It's not as fizzy tasting. Now feel the ginger at the back of the throat. It takes a bit longer to appear. It's very subtle, it's very nice, I really like it. I definitely buy Fentimans and I definitely buy the Double Dutch as well. They have different properties, different styles, different types of fizz. One has more sugar than the other and I'd give them both a five out of five.
transcriptUserEnriched.summary: The reviewer tests and compares Double Dutch Ginger Beer and Fentimans Classic Ginger Beer as mixers with rhubarb and ginger liqueur and lime. They find Double Dutch to be light, bubbly, and intense in ginger flavor, with a pleasant fizz. In contrast, Fentimans is described as more subtle, with a less intense flavor and fizz. Both drinks are rated five out of five, with the reviewer appreciating their distinct properties and styles.

transcriptUserEnriched.transcriptText: Today's head to head mixer challenge for Watch Humans. I'm doing tonic waters, Indian tonic waters. I've got a Fentimans and I've got a Double Dutch. And I'm gonna have a taste of these and then at the end I'll tell you which one I like the best. So I'm going to start with the Fentimans, which is nice big bottle. Thank you very much, watch humans. So have a little taste of that. It's lovely. That's really nice. I like that a lot. So this is the Double Dutch. It's a little bit more sparkly looking than the other one, but it's the taste we're after, not the appearance. And that's really nice. That's very nice. Something in that. There's something slightly spicy that I like in that, which yeah. I really go for this. Yum. Gosh. So made my decision, and based on that slight spiciness that I've not encountered with tonic water before, I'm gonna choose the Double Dutch. Really think it's very nice. And I've tasted both of these, just as tonic water as as a soft drink. I would normally have them with gin or vodka or whatever, but I wanted to taste the actual tonic water to see what that was like, because I think anything like gin and things will detract from the flavor. Very, very nice. I like the Double Dutch, and, it's not a brand I've encountered before, and I'm I'm gonna be looking out for it in the shops.
transcriptUserEnriched.summary: The reviewer conducted a taste test of two Indian tonic waters: Fentimans and Double Dutch. They found both to be enjoyable, but ultimately preferred Double Dutch for its unique slight spiciness. The reviewer appreciated the taste of both tonic waters on their own, without mixing them with alcohol, and expressed a desire to find Double Dutch in stores in the future.

transcriptUserEnriched.transcriptText: So today, I'm gonna be trying the Double Dutch Refresh Refreshing Lemonade. Can we take a moment to look at how lovely this bottle is? I really love ribbed designs in general, and I love the way that it's, ribbed but swirly pattern. I've never drunk from this brand before and it does say refreshing lemonade. So let's see if it really is as refreshing as it says. So let's give it I love the fact that it's really fizzy. Let's give it a try. Really refreshing. I don't know if it's because of the ice, but it's very not very lemony. I don't know if the ice has diluted it, but it's definitely refreshing, but it doesn't taste like pure lemons. Yeah. Interesting. But then again, it may be the fact that there's probably very little or no sugar in there. But, yeah, I think it's quite nice, but I am gonna give it, like, a average three out of five just because it isn't as lemony as I'd like it. But, yeah, really, really love the packaging.
transcriptUserEnriched.summary: The reviewer tries the Double Dutch Refresh Refreshing Lemonade and appreciates the attractive ribbed bottle design. They find the drink fizzy and refreshing but note that it lacks a strong lemon flavor, possibly due to dilution from ice or low sugar content. Overall, they rate it a 3 out of 5, considering it average due to the flavor not meeting their expectations, despite their fondness for the packaging.

transcriptUserEnriched.transcriptText: Hi, everyone. Abby here, and it is currently the evening. I have had a hectic day. I've just been to the gym, and as it is Saturday night, I thought I'm not going out. The weather's rubbish outside. It's absolutely pouring rain outside. I'm gonna make a nice alcoholic drink. So I'm gonna have a sweet flavor tonight. I'm gonna try something different. I'm gonna try two different ones and see which is better. So the first mix I'm gonna try with my gin drink is this from Double Dutch, and it is a pink grapefruit soft drink mixer. So it's low in calories as well, and it's gonna have a really nice sweet taste. And I love the bottle. I love the way it looks. And in the second drink, we've also got another pink grapefruit mix there, but this is from London Essence. So this is a crafted soda. So we're gonna see how this one pans out and yeah, it's gonna be interesting. So the first one I'm gonna try out is the London Essence one. Double Dutch one, I'm gonna use a bottle opener for this one. Oh god. Dropping it everywhere. But look at this. I absolutely love this on top. It's got a little dragonfly. She tries the London Essence crafted soda first, noting its strong grapefruit flavor and fizz, followed by the Double Dutch mixer, which she finds less fizzy but still enjoyable. Abby appreciates the aesthetics of both drinks, especially the bottle designs, and expresses enthusiasm for the flavors and colors of the mixers.

transcriptUserEnriched.transcriptText: Hello. I'm here to do a taste test. I'm gonna try two mixers, so two different lemonades in my vodka. Normally, I just buy whatever cheap lemonade. I normally buy the two litre bottle and I never drink it all. I have to throw it away because it's gone flat. So we've got these little bottles here to try. There's some premium double Dutch lemonade, which looks really posh, and then we've got the cute little schweppes can. So I'm gonna pour my drinks. Sorry. I don't have two of the same glasses apart from wine glasses. So I'm gonna stick my vodka in a minute and see which tastes the best. Just a little bit because it is the daytime. Yeah. So we're gonna make sure the same amount in each. That's the Swaps one. Here's the Double Dutch premium lemonade. About the same? Oh, right. Just give them a little stir. Gonna try a little bit on their own. Now the swept one is really sweet. Double Dutch, not as sweet. But I think I do prefer this one in vodka. The Double Dutch is slightly fizzier, and you can taste that when you're having the vodka. I think the schweppes is too sweet, so it takes the taste of the vodka away. So in my eyes, the Double Dutch is the winner. Now I've never seen this in the shops before, not that I've gone looking for it, so might have to look out for it and see. It's a cute little bottle, so I'm having a few drinks in the evening. That's it. Bottle done. No wastage. Nothing to go flat, so I might give it a go.
transcriptUserEnriched.summary: The reviewer conducted a taste test comparing two lemonades, Double Dutch premium lemonade and Schweppes lemonade, mixed with vodka. They noted that the Schweppes was too sweet and masked the vodka's flavor, while the Double Dutch was less sweet and slightly fizzier, making it the preferred choice. The reviewer expressed interest in purchasing the Double Dutch lemonade in the future due to its appealing packaging and the convenience of smaller bottles to avoid wastage.

transcriptUserEnriched.transcriptText: Hi, so I'm doing a sober night out and so my drink of choice today is the ginger beer. I don't think I've ever actually had ginger beer but we're gonna try. About strong. I wasn't expecting it to be that strong. I do quite like it though. It kind of makes me feel because obviously everyone else is drinking and it kind of makes me feel like I walk around with this bottle. Like I'm Part of the fun. I think it's the right level of fizzy as well. I would buy that. I quite like it.
transcriptUserEnriched.summary: The reviewer tries ginger beer for the first time during a sober night out and finds it surprisingly strong but enjoyable. They appreciate the taste, likening it to ginger shots they've had before, and feel that carrying the bottle allows them to feel included in the social atmosphere. They also note that the drink has the right level of fizziness and express a willingness to purchase it again.

transcriptUserEnriched.transcriptText: Hi, everyone. Abby here. I've just finished work, and I'm finally having a nice drink. So I'm gonna try something out today. It's in different well, the same flavor, but two different brands. We're gonna see which is better. Let's go. So I have my assistant Nelly here. And first, we have got the by Double Dutch, the cucumber and watermelon flavor premium mixer. Nelly says this should be good. And then the next one I've got is by Franklin and Sons and it's an elderflower and cucumber tonic water. So this will be interesting to see which tastes nicer. Let's go. So it's to clarify, obviously these two both carbonated drinks. I'm gonna make the same identical drink. So I'm gonna open these now. And I'm also gonna put in the same spirit. So I'm gonna use woah. That flew off. So I'm gonna use gin to put in both of them. Only a single because you know it is a school night. So I am going to add the spirits first. So literally only a single. It's really refreshing like this in summer would be amazing. So next we've got the elderflower and cucumber. Oh, okay. These are really nice. I think I prefer elderflower and cucumber. Not gonna lie. I love elderflower and elderflower comes in really nice and elderflower and cucumber are really good match. I mean yeah. I'm sorry. The Franklin is someone wins for me. transcriptUserEnriched.transcriptText: Hi everyone. Today we're going for the Double Dutch cucumber and watermelon tonic water. This is low in calories. It has that nice fizz to it. It pours very lightly. The bubbles sort of disappear quite quickly. Well, as I drank that the smell essence and taste of watermelon is number one here. It is really, really strong and powerful and I love watermelon so that's fantastic for me. It has that very fizzy hard sparkly before and after taste. The bubbles fizz on your tongue really well and then disappear. It's quite pleasant. A strong taste of cucumber I can taste there. But I like it. Very fizzy on the tongue. I'd give this a four out of five.

So I'm gonna give it a three out of five stars. I definitely can see it being a product for someone out there, someone enjoying it on a hot day. It is better than what I expected, which is a plus. I do appreciate the experience which I got. Unfortunately, yes, it's not for me.

transcriptUserEnriched.transcriptText: It's my day off today, and I'm about to go in the gym and do a major cardio session. After my workout, I'll definitely be needing a drink for sure, so I thought I would try the Fixate Kombucha Live Sparkling Soda, and this is in flavor gin gin. Gin. I've got gin in the brain. This is ginger and turmeric. So we'll give this a bash when I finish my workout. So I'll see yous in a bit. Really excited to try this kombucha drink as I'm really into my health and fitness, and I know kombucha is so good for your gut health, your microbiome and obviously fighting away bugs and stuff like that and it's got live culture which is your gut health again and it's low calories which is obviously a bonus as well. And then on to the workout. So I just started off with a slight jog just to obviously get my heart rate up. And this is a new plan that I've actually been doing so these moves are all pretty new to me. The one move that I'm still struggling with and getting used to is wall walks. You're supposed to have your spine like aligned against the wall. So this is a bit of a struggle but it's all new so I'm just getting used to it and obviously trying to get the form right as well. But so far so good. I'm definitely loving this new plan and all these different moves that I'm doing but yeah, just a wee bit of abs just to finish. And this was the workout. It was just upper and abs today. And then just to finish some flutter kicks and then some butterfly crunchies as well just to finish. So now at this point, I'm definitely looking forward to trying this drink. I always make sure there's no one in the changing rooms when I do these types of videos. Having a wee boogie because the music was on. But yeah, overall the taste test honestly it was delicious like I thought it was a really really nice drink. There wasn't like a weird aftertaste or anything like that to it. To be honest, I'm not really into fizzy drinks but this beats like a Diet Coke or Iron Brew any day because it tasted just as good to be honest and it's healthy. So yeah, I would rate this a five out of five.

transcriptUserEnriched.transcriptText: So I'm gonna be trying the Fix8 Water Kefir. One is raspberry lemonade and the other is peach and mango. So these are live sparkling sodas, a lot of the sugar and it's low calories as well. So it's basically like a guilt free drink, dairy free and vegan, real fruit, so there's none of that fake rubbish, live cultures, which is really good for your gut, raw and unpasteurized. And I just want to say like the can is so so eye catching. I love how they've designed it, because I would definitely look at that if I seen that in store. First of all, smells delish. That's not what I expected, actually. It reminds me of a sort of like peach orange flavored yogurt, but in like a drink form, but that is really nice, like very refreshing and you can tell obviously like it is healthy. I've actually got some work to do, so I'm gonna sit and drink the rest of it and then throughout the week we'll test this other one. So I'm on day two, we're on Wednesday and I'm gonna be trying the raspberry lemonade. And I thoroughly enjoyed the mango peach one, that was really nice. There's not major flavor going on with this one, I would say. You get the hint of raspberry, little bit of lemonade but it's not super strong. Out of the two, I would say I prefer the mango peach one, that was definitely my favorite. I would rate that probably a four out of five just because I wasn't overly keen on this flavor but they are pretty nice. Yeah, for me I would swap this. I don't actually drink a lot of fizzy juice to be honest but when I do it's always Iron Brew Extra, so knowing that this is full of goodness is definitely an easy swap for me. If you're looking to maybe come off fizzy juice or just look for a healthier drink, you're not too keen on water, I would highly recommend trying this.

The peach mango flavor is praised for its refreshing taste, while the raspberry lemonade is noted to be less flavorful. The reviewer prefers the peach mango, rating it 4 out of 5, and recommends the product for those looking for a healthier alternative to sugary sodas.

transcriptUserEnriched.transcriptText: Hi guys. Today I'm trying the Fix8 water kefir with peach and mango. It's a live sparkling soda and what am I expecting from kefir? Well, I drink bottled yogurt Kefir so if it's similar to that then obviously it's gonna be sparkling just as a Kefir yogurt would have that sparkling taste so expecting that because it's a sparkling drink. I'm expecting a nice peach and mango taste, no added sugars and I'm expecting it to be healthy you know good ingredients low in calories for example. Wow. There's a very sort of sour on the tongue aftertaste which is a good sour. It's like you know when you eat one of those fizzy sweets and can just feel at the sides of your tongue that sourness but it's very nice. Perhaps it's the live cultures I don't know. It's a lovely lovely flavour. It's dairy free, vegan free, real fruit formation. It's raw and unpasteurised and I guess I expected Kefir to be just like that. I have never tried Kefir in a drink before and I'll definitely be swapping my usual drinks for something like this. It's really really really nice. How does it compare with other Kefir drinks? Well I've never really tried Kefir in a soda. I would probably keep buying yogurt Kefir because I like it and it's healthy and it's good for your gut. And like I said I really like this because it's nicely fizzy it's not over fizzy you know the bubbles don't go up your nose. I don't know if I dare say but I can actually taste similarities to the yogurt kefir in the sense that there's that you know bubbliness, that sparkling dare I say yogurt like taste? Maybe I've just got it in my brain that I'm tasting yogurt kefir but it's so similar I can't explain it. So would I recommend it? Yes. Absolutely to anyone that loves kefir and to anyone that just wants a healthy gut, wants no added sugar and wants to stay healthy. So Fix8, I really love you and your recyclable cans. Thank you.

The reviewer appreciates the drink's fizziness and health benefits, stating they would recommend it to others who enjoy kefir or seek healthy options. They express a strong liking for the product and commend its packaging.

transcriptUserEnriched.transcriptText: I just got back from The US. Not only am I exhausted, but I also have no food, which is not a good place to be in. Going to go to Sainsbury's for a meal deal. It's actually so nice today. I got a yummy little wrap, some pineapple and mango, and then a fix eight kombucha. And I've had this before, and this is honestly perfect for what I'm looking for today because it has a little bit of caffeine, and it hits the spot. I feel like the flavor that I've chosen for the kombucha works well for different reasons with both of the things that I picked. I think it helps, like, lighten all of the cheese and saltiness of the wrap, but it complements the mango and pineapple flavors. Very satisfied. This might be my new go to.

transcriptUserEnriched.transcriptText: I'm excited to try Fix It's Kombucha with the ginger and turmeric. I never was really a Kombucha drink. I didn't like it that much. And when I started doing Watch Humans Try It, I tried the Momo raspberry and hibiscus one. And I thought, thought, this is actually a much more delicious drink than I was expecting. So I started looking for it in the stores and actually, Marks and Spencer do a ginger one that I really like. So I thought, right, I'm gonna try this one with the ginger and turmeric. I started taking turmeric because of its anti inflammatory properties. I was feeling a bit of nausea and things and it was recommended by the doctors. Right brilliant ginger, turmeric, kombucha, what a brilliant thing to grab quickly at lunchtime while all the kids are outside. I normally would be teaching, so it's just a nice little treat in the middle of a rather busy Monday. So let's see what this tastes like. Oh, yes. Really delicious. Actually, that's probably the nicest kombucha I've tasted so far. It's really fresh, almost apple juice flavor and the ginger and turmeric black pepper is not too overpowering. Also tells me that 25% of my daily vitamin C is in here, so it's immune supporting, the live bacterial cultures are there, which helps to maintain gut health. And then obviously for me, turmeric with the black pepper and the ginger are really anti inflammatory and good for your general gut health. Great company, started in 2018 in London and they're working hard to make good quality natural products, vegan friendly, obviously, and only 38 calories per can. So it is a genuine low calorie treat which is gonna really cheer up my Monday lunch. I would definitely recommend it. Well done, Fixate. And enjoy the rest of this. Five stars.
transcriptUserEnriched.summary: The reviewer expresses excitement about trying Fix It's Kombucha with ginger and turmeric, noting a previous positive experience with kombucha. They appreciate the drink's taste, describing it as fresh and flavorful, and highlight its health benefits, including immune support and gut health. The reviewer praises the company's commitment to quality and low-calorie options, ultimately giving the product a five-star rating and recommending it.

transcriptUserEnriched.transcriptText: Hello. So today is a work from home day. And as you can see, it is very gloomy and horrible outside. Normally, the weather gets like this, I tend to get a bit of sniffles, I can tell that the cold is probably about to come. So what better time than to try Fixate Kombucha in ginger and turmeric? I'm always trying to find lower sugar, lower calorie alternatives since I've just started going back to the gym again. And this can actually has 25% of your daily vitamin C as well. Ginger and turmeric, as we know, is really good for its anti inflammatory and, immune health benefits. It's also got, black pepper, green tea, ginger root, and turmeric. So all the things that are really good for your immune system. So we're gonna give this a go today. It's also only got 38 calories per can. So let's give this a try. Oh, very gingery, quite fizzy. It's got a nice clear color. Let's let's try. Oh, that's really gingery. I wouldn't quite say it says gingery is like a ginger beer, but you can really taste that ginger. It's got a nice sweetness, and it actually had doesn't have much of that funky kombucha taste with that I've tried with other, kombuchas before. This is really nice. I would actually pick this is something that I would actually pick up in a shop. I would I'm always trying new things. So, things like this wouldn't wouldn't throw me off. I would 100% pick this up and try it, and I would definitely buy this if I saw it in the in the shop. Live cultures, so really good for gut health as well. Like I said, vitamin c is really, really high in the vitamins that you need for the day. Sorry. I'm just trying to read this at the same time. Yeah. Low calories, plant based as well, 100% natural. It's got a sweetness, but without being sickly sweet or overly sweet. They haven't added loads and loads of sugar to it. It's actually only got 7.8 grams of sugar per can. So that's not too bad in comparison to other fizzy drinks that you might pick up. I'm really enjoying this.

The reviewer expresses a strong likelihood of purchasing this product again, highlighting its natural ingredients and gut health benefits.

transcriptUserEnriched.transcriptText: My name's Carla and I'm here on holiday in York. I've just walked seven miles sightseeing around the beautiful city and now I've come out of the hustle and bustle, found us somewhere to sit and I'm just relaxing by the minster which you can see behind me. I thought this would be a perfect place to try strawberry and basil kombucha by Fixate. It's a drink that's good for your immune health and also it's got life cultures which help your gut. As someone who suffers from coeliac disease, I thought this would be perfect because I need help to improve my gut and also to make it healthy. Let's see what it tastes like. That's delicious. You can really taste the strawberry very strong. It's not too sweet. It's a bit fizzy, but not overwhelmingly fizzy. And you can taste a little hint of basil. I've only tried kombucha once before, which was apple flavored, and I love that as well. This is just as good, and I think I would definitely give it five out of five. It's really helping me to relax. You've just taken it off me. So zero feet. It looks like my son's just stolen it.
transcriptUserEnriched.summary: Carla, on holiday in York, reviews Fixate's strawberry and basil kombucha while relaxing by the minster. She appreciates its health benefits, especially for her coeliac disease, and finds the taste delicious, noting strong strawberry flavor with a hint of basil. She rates it five out of five and mentions it helps her relax, although her son steals her drink during the review.

transcriptUserEnriched.transcriptText: I am a pretty big kombucha drinker. Ginger and lemon is, like, my favorite flavor combination, so it is my go to for kombuchas. I'm going to be making a mocktail tonight with the Fixate Kombucha, ginger, and turmeric. I don't drink alcohol, so I am always happy just having like fun little kombuchas and mocktails and things like that. Because I don't drink and I never really have my whole life, I don't really know how to make cocktails, how to mix things together in a good way. So this is going to be a very, very experimental It's Friday and I just got home, so I'm just I'm gonna clean my room and put on a movie, and I just want, like, a little treat myself kind of thing to pair along with that. I had a couple things in my fridge. Because I'm so busy, I never really have tons of time to go to the store. So I'm kind of just gonna make a concoction of different things that I have. But I think that they will go well. I'm gonna try it on its own first so I can get an idea of the flavor profile. Oh, I love this. It tastes almost like a soda. It almost doesn't even taste like super strong of kombucha, like it doesn't have the taste of like the mother. I'm gonna start by putting the drink in. I am a girl who always gets UTIs, always have cranberry juice on hand, and I thought that a bit of cranberry juice could actually be quite nice. I'm gonna put some just a little bit because it's quite strong. I am obsessed with citrus. Just love that feeling of sourness in my mouth. So I've got lemon, cranberry juice, and the fix eight kombucha in here for my little mocktail. Cheers. It's really nice. This is the perfect little cocktail for my Friday night. I would give this a five out of five, and I would definitely buy this again if I saw it in the shop, and I would definitely recommend this to friends who are, like, wanting to see what they think about kombucha. Love it so much.
transcriptUserEnriched.summary: The reviewer, a kombucha enthusiast, shares their experience of making a mocktail using Fixate Kombucha, ginger, turmeric, and cranberry juice. They describe their love for the ginger and lemon flavor combination and express excitement about creating a drink without alcohol. After tasting the kombucha, they find it enjoyable and soda-like, and they rate their mocktail a perfect five out of five, expressing a desire to buy it again and recommend it to friends.

It's got life cultures, immune health, low calories. The best drink for summer, actually.

transcriptUserEnriched.transcriptText: Watch me try the fix eight kombucha strawberry basil. I am taking this out with me in my back garden because it's a little bit sunny today. I'm really, really excited to try this because I find fizzy drinks a little bit too sweet these days, and I've heard that kombucha is really, really good for you. I am always up to trying healthy stuff anytime. I've also tried kombucha in the past, and if I'm gonna be honest, it just tasted very bland. Immediately got a whiff of strawberry in my face. Let's try it together. Cheers. Oh, okay. Now this tastes just like strawberry soda. This is 10 times better than the kombucha I tried in the past. Summery, sweet, fresh, so full of strawberries. I cannot taste the basil in this, but I can 100% taste the strawberry in this. And that's fine with me because who doesn't love strawberries? Also low calories and made with a 100% natural ingredients. For those who say that healthy sodas aren't nice, bland, and are not sweet, try this. Just try it. Considering this supports the immune health, I would a 100% ditch the fizzy drinks and switch to kombucha, especially this one because it's really good. Fix a just fix my problems.

The drink is praised for its summery, sweet, and fresh strawberry flavor, while the basil is not noticeable. The reviewer appreciates its low calorie count and natural ingredients, recommending it as a healthier alternative to sugary sodas, especially due to its immune health benefits.

transcriptUserEnriched.transcriptText: Just got back from Sainsbury's. I've been out picking up my lunch for the day. I thought I'd go for my usual wrap, the sausage roll, but I'm also trying the Sicilian citrus fix eight kombucha. We're gonna try this out and see how it goes. So, usually, I'd go for, like, an energy drink or something. But I'm thinking maybe I have too much caffeine in my diet, so this could be a good replacement. I really like the flavor. I think it works really well with the kind of very subtle spice of the Korean chicken. I don't usually think that I'm a kombucha fan. Maybe I just haven't tried enough kombucha, but this was really good. If you just gave me a can of this, I'm not sure I'd be able to tell you that it was kombucha. It just kind of tastes like a sweet drink, which is usually what I'm looking for. So I definitely get this again. I think it'd be hard to replace the the caffeine hit of an energy drink that I usually like around this time, but it's definitely good, and it's definitely something that I would get again.
transcriptUserEnriched.summary: The reviewer shares their experience trying a Sicilian citrus fix eight kombucha from Sainsbury's, which they chose as a potential replacement for their usual energy drink. They express a positive surprise at the flavor, noting it pairs well with their lunch and doesn't taste like typical kombucha. The reviewer appreciates the drink's sweetness and indicates they would purchase it again, despite acknowledging the challenge of replacing the caffeine boost from energy drinks.

transcriptUserEnriched.transcriptText: I'm just about to have my lunch, so I thought now would be the perfect time to test out the Fix8 Water Kefir. This is a raspberry lemonade flavored live sparkling soda. Let's give it a go. Oh, it's quite smooth. It's not as bubbly as I thought it was gonna be. That's really nice. It's really sweet. It's a little bit tangy, but really nice. It reminds me a little bit of cream soda. It's that sort of flavor. I really like that. I love the packaging. It's also dairy free and vegan, real fruit, low calories, live cultures. I really, really like this. This isn't a product I'd normally pick up, but actually I love raft lemonade. This tastes really nice. It's a much nicer both having it than some of the other cans on the market, so I'm really impressed.

The reviewer expresses surprise at enjoying a product they wouldn't typically choose, noting it is superior to other similar products on the market.

transcriptUserEnriched.transcriptText: Hello. Today I'm gonna be testing the fixate kombucha. I've been working from home the entire day for uni. I've been working for four hours and usually when I'm done about that time for grabbing lunch, I always get myself a diet coke. I'm kind of addicted to sodas in general. I'm always trying to drink sodas without sugar. For this huge craving, I really need a sparkling drink. I really wanna cut it. I wanna start drinking coke for loads of different reasons. And I've been hunting for a new drink. I'm really excited to try this. Oh my god. This really smells amazing. You can really already smell the strawberry and even the basil, which is super impressive. Cheers. This is really, really good. Usually, I'm not a huge fan of kombucha because you have, like, this fermented taste that I don't really enjoy. But you still have a bit of like something a bit sour in the aftertaste. But beside that, you can actually taste the strawberry. You can taste the basil. Lightly sweet, but not too much. And I love how sparkly that is. It's not too much either, but just just perfect. Honestly honestly, I would buy this again. Really low sugar as well and all natural ingredients. This is really good. I love fixate. If you want to still drink sodas, but you're quite aware of what you're drinking and you want to make a safer choice for your body and environment, I think this is a great option and I would definitely buy it again. I give it a five out of five really. Cheers.
transcriptUserEnriched.summary: The reviewer tests Fixate Kombucha after working from home and expresses a desire to replace soda with a healthier option. They are pleasantly surprised by the drink's aroma and flavor, noting the strong presence of strawberry and basil, and appreciate its low sugar content and natural ingredients. Despite typically disliking the fermented taste of kombucha, they find this one enjoyable and declare they would buy it again, rating it five out of five.

transcriptUserEnriched.transcriptText: Hi, I've been giving this drink to try Fix 8, Kombucha, Sicilian Citrus, Immune Health which is great because I work with children and disability people so I get an awful lot of colds. So it looks really nice. I've left it in the fridge so it's nice and cold. It's low calories as well and as if you've seen any of my other videos I'm on a low calorie diet so this fits in just well. It's got vitamin C. Fantastic. Right, let's give it a go. Cheers! Very fizzy, which suits me. Quite orangey. Not too citrusy. It's not lemony. I thought it was gonna have a bitter bite to it, but it hasn't. Actually quite a soft flavor. So try it for yourself. I'm not sure where I stand. I'm on the fence if I like it a lot or if I don't like it. It's it's nothing spectacular. But would I have it again? Yeah. I probably would.
transcriptUserEnriched.summary: The reviewer discusses their experience with Fix 8 Kombucha in Sicilian Citrus flavor, highlighting its low calorie content, vitamin C inclusion, and refreshing taste. They appreciate its fizziness and soft flavor, noting it is not overly citrusy or bitter. However, they express uncertainty about their overall enjoyment, stating they are on the fence about liking it a lot but would consider having it again.

transcriptUserEnriched.transcriptText: So today I'm going to be comparing the fixate kombucha that's ginger turmeric flavoured with the remedy kombucha which is ginger and lemon. Now I love ginger drinks so hopefully I will decide which one I like. I've had the fixate kombucha before, I really like the flavour of this. It's so fiery! That's the perfect level of ginger I like and the turmeric adds extra fire to it which I also really love. It's quite fizzy which is expected for kombucha but it's not too sweet. So next the remedy kombucha, the lemon and ginger. That one's quite tart and I wasn't expecting it to taste like that and I'm not getting as much of the fire from the ginger. I'm gonna have to say I really prefer the fixate kombucha on this one. It was much better flavour and the ginger came through so well.
transcriptUserEnriched.summary: The reviewer compares two kombucha drinks: Fixate's ginger turmeric flavor and Remedy's ginger lemon flavor. They express a strong preference for the Fixate kombucha, praising its fiery ginger flavor and the added kick from turmeric, while finding the Remedy kombucha too tart and lacking in ginger intensity.

transcriptUserEnriched.transcriptText: Good afternoon. I'm going to be testing these two kombuchas. I'm sat in my home office, and I need a drink. So I thought I'd put these two to the test. So here we have fixate kombucha in psyllium citrus, and I'm looking forward to trying this. It's got live cultures and is low in calories. Let me give it a taste. Oh, yes. I can definitely taste the sunshine in this drink, the Sicilian orange. It's very refreshing. It's not too tart. It's not too sweet. It's very, very nice. The next one to try is naturally Nexpa sugar free kombucha mixed berry. There we go. Slightly bigger can. Let's give this one a taste. Oh, as you could tell from my face. It's got it's quite tart, And it leaves a little bit of a bitter taste afterwards. I think it's probably because it's that sugar free stuff, she says. Gonna give it another try just in case. No. I'm not overly keen on that one at all. I don't think I'd get that one again. However, this one, the fix eight, that's really refreshing. So from my two that I've tested, I would definitely recommend this one. If you like something that is a little bit citrus, quite light, very refreshing. Fixate wins. Have a lovely afternoon.
transcriptUserEnriched.summary: The reviewer tests two kombucha drinks: Fixate Kombucha in Psyllium Citrus and Naturally Nexpa Sugar Free Kombucha in Mixed Berry. The Fixate Kombucha is described as refreshing, not too tart or sweet, and is recommended. In contrast, the Nexpa Kombucha is noted to be quite tart with a bitter aftertaste, and the reviewer does not recommend it. Overall, the Fixate Kombucha is favored over the Nexpa.

transcriptUserEnriched.transcriptText: Now I don't know about you but I have quite a stressful job and I need to have a hearty meal to keep me going but I also like to appreciate the finer things in life like pretty flowers and some delicious fruit and well, I love a little sweet treat every now and then and this is where Ombar Blonde's chocolate comes in and today's focus, the fixate kombucha. It's great to have something to fixate on and what better than to fixate on this lovely new kombucha drink. Kombucha can taste awful at times, you have to admit, but these new brands that are coming out with fancy flavored versions of it, they are doing a remarkable job of bringing kombucha to the mainstream. And this mixture of strawberry and basil is just quite sublime actually. It's the sort of thing that I would not expect would go very well together at all. When I saw a strawberry basil, I was wondering if this would just taste quite medicinal but no indeed, there is a very gentle flavor of strawberry with almost a sort of a a little spicy aftertaste which must be the basil. This is live sparkling soda. So it's full of live cultures, it's low in calories, it's great for your immune health, it also is 25% of your daily vitamin c intake and it's plant based so it's vegan friendly. I just love the fact that this refreshing strawberry basil drink from fixate takes summery strawberry and pairs it with crisp basil to give a fruity refreshing and gut loving kombucha. And I have to agree with that tagline made right here in The UK. Fixate kombucha sodas are made from 100% natural ingredients loaded with vitamin c and live cultures that are scientifically designed to reach your gut. As they say on the packaging, cool. I And definitely agree. Stay bold and drink cold. That is the tagline. I have to say fixate kombucha strawberry basil is very delicious indeed and both my wife and I thoroughly enjoyed having a little sip together on a nice sunny afternoon relaxing in the conservatory. And I know that you will enjoy it too. I can't wait to try some of Fixate's other flavors.
transcriptUserEnriched.summary: The video review discusses the Fixate Kombucha in the flavor of strawberry and basil, highlighting its refreshing taste and health benefits. The reviewer appreciates the combination of flavors, noting that it is surprisingly pleasant and not medicinal as initially feared. The drink is praised for being low in calories, vegan-friendly, and rich in vitamin C and live cultures, making it beneficial for gut health. The reviewer and their wife enjoyed the drink together, expressing excitement to try more flavors from Fixate.

assumptions: Transcript-user enrichment uses the documented composite key (reviewId, personId).
Products are retained even when they have no transcript evidence so competitor context remains visible.
Brands with no reviews are labelled Competitive context rather than treated as data-quality failures.
Theme extraction is transparent keyword matching over transcript text, not a model-inferred truth label.

Transcript signals are deterministic phrase matches with evidence snippets across praise, pain points, occasions, shopper needs, product preferences, routines, co-consumption, purchase drivers, complaints, retailers, competitors, and comparisons.
Signal confidence combines matched phrase count with simple supporting evidence from rating, sentiment, and would-buy response.
Commercial aggregates report both raw mention counts and normalized rates so groups with different transcript coverage can be compared cautiously.
Positioning vs Reviewer Reality compares structured product/brand keywords with mapped transcript signals; it should be read as directional evidence, not statistical proof.
